title: Real Estate Virtual Assistant
description:
About Us: The Link Housing is a dynamic and rapidly growing real estate company dedicated to providing exceptional rental properties to our clients. We pride ourselves on our commitment to excellence and our passion for helping individuals and families find their perfect home.



Job Description:

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to join our team. As a Virtual Assistant, you will play a key role in our real estate operations by assisting with the acquisition of homes for rent. This is a remote position with flexible hours within the Pacific Time Zone.



Responsibilities:



Make phone calls to potential homeowners to inquire about listing their properties for rent.

Gather and organize relevant information using Google Sheets.

Assist with administrative tasks and documentation related to property acquisition.

Provide excellent customer service to homeowners and potential clients.



Key Responsibilities:



Source suitable housing options within a 10-mile radius of the displaced residence using platforms like Airbnb, Zillow, and Furnished Finder.

Utilize Google Maps to center searches on target locations, ensuring proximity, quality, and availability.

Conduct outbound calls to property owners and landlords to negotiate rental agreements and advocate for displaced families.

Submit accurate, comprehensive property listings for internal review, including pricing, amenities, location, and owner contact information.

Maintain a high standard of professionalism in written and spoken English when communicating with landlords and internal teams.

Accurately log and track submissions and activities using Google Sheets, Google Docs, and 
.

Work efficiently in a fast-paced environment, balancing speed, accuracy, and customer satisfaction.

Apply negotiation and sales skills to encourage property owners to list their homes for temporary housing.



Key Skills & Requirements:

Proficiency with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Maps)

Strong experience using OTA (Online Travel Agency) platforms: Airbnb, Furnished Finder, Zillow

Excellent understanding of geolocation and map-based search techniques

Strong communication skills – both verbal and written (professional English)

Ability to work independently and manage time across multiple cases

Knowledge of 
 or similar project management tools

Sales and negotiation experience is essential

Must be available to work Pacific Standard Time (PST) hours



Ideal Candidate:



You are tech-savvy, detail-focused, and mission-driven. You’re passionate about helping families in need, and you thrive in a remote, fast-paced environment where accuracy, speed, and empathy matter. You’re also proactive, highly organized, and confident in negotiating terms with landlords.



Requirements:

Bachelor's degree in a related field.

Effective negotiations experience

Strong communication skills and comfortable making phone calls.

Proficiency in Google Sheets or similar spreadsheet software.

Detail-oriented with excellent organizational skills.

Ability to work independently and prioritize tasks effectively.



Benefits:



Competitive salary based on experience with Monthly Bonus Opportunity

Remote work opportunity with the potential for growth within the company.



Please review the application details below. Applications with missing requirements will not be considered.



1. Submit your resume and a cover letter outlining your qualifications and why you would be a great fit for this position.



2. Include a photo of your computer set up. In the photo, please have 
 opened on your computer Monitor. In your cover letter, please change the font color of the date to red. Lastly, please highlight the last sentence of the cover letter in yellow.



3. Lastly, please upload a 1 minute video or voice recording introducing yourself.





We thank all applicants for their interest in joining The Link Housing, however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

title: Outreach Virtual Assistant — DrRomulusMBA (LinkedIn DMs + GHL Pipeline)
description:
About us




anna Romulus helps service-based founders — consultants, coaches, and agency owners — install the systems behind predictable revenue. Our methodology is the DDS Framework: Diagnose, Design, Scale. Structure before scale.



The role



You will be the first touchpoint for our Revenue Architecture Session pipeline. Your job is to put qualified founders on Dr. Romulus's calendar — using personalized outbound, clean follow-up, and disciplined CRM hygiene. This is a conversion role, not a content role. We measure in booked calls, not messages sent.



What you will own



1. Outbound DM and
 — 20 to 40 personalized first-touches per day across LinkedIn (primary) and
, plus a structured 2-step follow-up sequence. Scripts will be approved and provided.



2. GHL pipeline management — every conversation logged in GoHighLevel, opportunity stages updated daily, booking link delivered at the right 
nt, no leads lost.



3. Lead research and list building — daily ICP prospect list of 20 to 50 profiles sourced from LinkedIn and Apollo, personalization angles documented before any outreach goes out.



Tools you will work in



GoHighLevel (GHL), LinkedIn (Sales Navigator helpful, not required), Apollo, Notion, Loom,
 or Slack, ChatGPT or Claude.



What we are looking for



— 2 or more years of appointment-setting or outbound work for service-based businesses, ideally coaches or consultants

— Native-level written English; your DM voice should be indistinguishable from a US-based founder

— LinkedIn outbound experience with a result you can name in numbers (X messages sent ? Y meetings booked)

— Comfort inside GHL or a comparable CRM with daily pipeline hygiene

— AI tool fluency (ChatGPT, Claude, Apollo) used in your real workflow today

— Direct-to-founder experience preferred — we hire the operator, not the agency



Compensation

$500 to $700 per month USD, paid monthly via Payoneer or PayPal (your choice). Final rate set in interview based on experience. 20 to 30 hours per week.



How to apply



Reply with three things:



1. One paragraph — why this role, specifically.

2. Your best appointment-setting result. Name the business, the channel, and the number.

3. Salary expectation and your weekly hours availability.



Applications without all three will not be reviewed.



Dr. Romulus

ANAMECHI Marketing

title: Video Editor - Real Estate
description:
To apply, complete the Google Form linked in this posting.





Applications submitted directly through OnlineJobs.ph will not be reviewed.



ABOUT ALLISON MEDIA CO.



Allison Media Co. is a premium real estate media company serving high-performing agents and developers across Ontario — from Niagara to Toronto. We produce photo, video, aerial, and marketing content that helps properties sell and brands stand out.



We're growing fast. Volume is up. Our standard is high. And we need editors who can keep up.



WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS



This isn't a filler role. We have real estate shoots happening daily and a growing backlog of video content that needs to be edited to a premium standard — fast.



The edit is where the brand lives. Every video we deliver goes to an agent's listing, a developer's launch, or a brand's social presence. Your work directly impacts how our clients are perceived and how our business grows.



We need someone who can jump in, learn the standard quickly, and start cutting clips that are clean, consistent, and on-brand — immediately.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING



This is a high-volume, high-standard editing role. You'll be responsible for:



Editing 3–5 real estate video packages per day

Working primarily in Premiere Pro and DaVinci Resolve

Following AMC's editing style, pacing, and color guidelines

Catching your own errors before files are submitted for review

Organizing and managing your own project files cleanly

Communicating clearly with the lead editor and owner on status and flags

Improving your output quality continuously based on feedback



HOW YOU WIN HERE



You'll know you're succeeding when:



Your edits are consistently clean and require minimal revisions

You're hitting turnaround targets without sacrificing quality

The team trusts your files without needing to double-check everything

Your output is improving week over week

Clients are proud to post what we deliver



REQUIREMENTS



Non-negotiable:



Real estate video editing experience — you must have a portfolio that proves it

Proficiency in Premiere Pro and/or DaVinci Resolve

Strong understanding of pacing, color, and storytelling for property video

Ability to work full-time immediately upon hiring

Reliable internet, computer, and professional editing setup

Strong written English for team communication

High attention to detail — you catch mistakes before anyone else does

Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously without losing quality



WHO THIS IS FOR



You've edited real estate content before and you're good at it

You work fast without getting sloppy

You take feedback seriously and apply it on the next edit, not the one after

You want consistent full-time volume, not sporadic freelance work

You're self-directed — you don't need to be told what to do next

You care about the quality of your work, not just getting files out the door



WHO THIS IS NOT FOR



Editors without real estate experience

Anyone who avoids feedback or gets defensive about revisions

People who ship 'good enough' work

Freelancers looking for passive, low-commitment work

Anyone who needs constant direction to stay on task



COMPENSATION



Starting Rate:

$6–$10 CAD per hour, based on experience and portfolio quality



Growth:

Performance-based — not time-based. Strong editors grow fast here.



OUR CORE VALUES



These are operating requirements — not wall art.



Extreme Ownership

We take full responsibility for outcomes. We fix problems before they reach the client, follow through, and don't make excuses.



Flexible Excellence

We adapt to real-world challenges without dropping our standard. Speed matters, but polish is non-negotiable.



Professional Confidence

We show up with calm, capable leadership. We guide the client, lead on-site, and carry ourselves like a part of their brand.



Sincere Accountability

We give and receive feedback with clarity, humility, and care. We call each other higher, not out.



HOW TO APPLY



Submit your application through the Google Form linked in this posting. Direct applications through OnlineJobs.ph will not be reviewed.



Your application must include:



Your best real estate video or demo reel (link required)

A 1–2 minute video introduction: who you are, what you're proud of, and why you're the right fit for AMC



We are not looking for the most experienced editor.

We are looking for the right one.



To apply, complete the Google Form linked in this posting.

title: Full-Stack AI Engineer Experienced to Develop AI based softwares and database
description:
We are looking for an exceptional AI Engineer to help us build a powerfull AI platform from the ground up.



Our vision is to create one central AI platform where our team, users, and customers can solve problems, automate work, generate content, analyze data, and access advanced AI capabilities without depending on multiple outside tools. We want to build a serious product with long-term potential.



We are looking for someone who is not just skilled, but deeply excited by the challenge.





This role is for someone who genuinely loves building, solving hard problems, and turning big ideas into real products. We can provide resources, tools, support, and room to build. What we need from you is strong engineering ability, deep curiosity, ownership, and the hunger to create something meaningful.

What We Want to Build



We want to build an AI platform that can eventually include



features like:

conversational AI

AI agents and workflows

document analysis and knowledge retrieval

content generation

business automation

internal team productivity tools

integrations with different tools, systems, and databases

private/company-specific AI usage in one ecosystem

scalable infrastructure for future growth

The long-term goal is to create a platform that reduces the need to jump between multiple AI tools and gives users one strong system to work from.



What You’ll Do

You will help lead the technical build of the platform, including:

designing the architecture for the AI platform

building AI-powered product features end-to-end

working on backend systems, APIs, databases, and model integration

helping define the product roadmap from an engineering perspective

building clean, scalable, maintainable systems

improving speed, accuracy, performance, and usability

creating secure and reliable workflows for users and internal teams

collaborating closely with leadership to turn vision into execution



What We’re Looking For

We want someone with strong experience in areas such as:

AI/ML engineering

LLM application development

full-stack product engineering

backend architecture and APIs

vector databases / retrieval systems / RAG

agent workflows and AI orchestration

model deployment and inference optimization

cloud infrastructure and scaling

product thinking and execution

You should be comfortable working with things like:

Python

FastAPI or similar backend frameworks

React / Next.js or similar frontend tools

Docker

cloud platforms

databases

AI infrastructure

open-source model ecosystems

performance optimization

security and reliability best practices

Ideal Candidate

You may be a great fit if you:

have built real AI products, not just demos

can think like both an engineer and a builder

enjoy solving difficult technical problems

can work independently and move fast

understand how to turn ambitious ideas into practical systems

are excited by building something big from the ground up

care deeply about quality, speed, and ownership

Important

This role is for builders.

If you only want routine tasks, this is definintely not the right fit.

If you get excited by hard challenges, big vision, product ownership, and the chance to help build something serious, we want to hear from you.

We are ready to support the right person with resources, tools, and long-term opportunity. We want someone who sees this as more than just another freelance gig.



To Apply

Please send:

a short introduction about yourself

your relevant AI/product engineering experience

links to platforms, products, or systems you’ve built

the AI stack/tools/frameworks you know best

an example of a difficult problem you solved

how you would approach building an all-in-one AI platform from scratch

why this opportunity interests you



Bonus Points

Extra strong if you have experience with:

private/self-hosted AI systems

enterprise AI tools

multi-agent systems

model fine-tuning

inference optimization

AI product architecture

SaaS product development

startup or founding engineer experience

title: Virtual Assistant (Sales-Focused)
description:
A growing U.S.-based cleaning company focused on delivering excellent service and consistent growth. We are expanding and need strong communicators who can help us convert leads into booked jobs.



We are not just looking for an assistant—we are looking for someone who is *hungry, driven, and motivated to make money through performance and commissions.*



---

This role is a mix of *Virtual Assistant + Sales Representative.*



You will be responsible for handling calls, following up with leads, and *turning inquiries into booked appointments.* If you are confident on the phone and enjoy closing deals, this role is for you.



---



*Work Schedule (CST – U.S. Time Zone)*



* Monday to Friday

* Training: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM CST

* After training: 8:00 AM – 1:00 PM CST

* 5 hours per day (part-time to start)

* Must be flexible—some days may require extended hours

* Opportunity to move into full-time based on performance



---



*Compensation*



* $5/hour during 2–3 week probation

* $6/hour after successful probation

* *Commission opportunities available based on bookings and performance*

* Increased pay and hours for high performers

---

*Key Responsibilities*



* Handle inbound and outbound calls

* Follow up with leads quickly and consistently

* Convert inquiries into booked cleaning appointments

* Schedule jobs and update customer records

* Deliver professional, confident, and friendly customer service

* Support admin tasks as needed



---



*What We’re REALLY Looking For*



* Strong *sales mindset* (this is NOT just admin work)

* Confident speaking on the phone and handling objections

* Experience with *cold calling, lead follow-up, or closing sales*

* Fast response time and strong follow-through

* Self-motivated and performance-driven

* Reliable, organized, and able to work independently



---

*BONUS (Not Required but Preferred)*



* Experience working with a U.S. cleaning company

* Experience in appointment setting or call center sales

---

*Important – Read Before Applying*



We ONLY consider applicants who follow instructions.



To apply, you MUST submit:



1. A *1–2 minute VIDEO recording* (not audio) answering:



   * Your sales experience

   * How you handle objections or difficult customers

   * Why you are a good fit for a sales-focused role



2. A link to your resume (*make sure access is public*)



*If you do NOT submit a video, your application will be automatically rejected.*



Start your application with: *“I’m ready to close deals”*



---



We are looking for someone who wants to grow, earn more, and be part of a fast-moving team—not someone just looking for a basic VA job.

title: Real Estate Dispositions Team member

We’re hiring a Dispositions Specialist to build out our dispositions program—with the guidance our leadership. You’ll be responsible for marketing all properties, engaging with our buyers via phone and text, and creating clear policies and SOPs for how our disposition machine should run.

We are an established real estate investment company with years of experience in acquisitions, creative deal structures, and on-market sales. We operate primarily in North Carolina and have a strong deal flow.

Our acquisition pipeline is built and proven. Our systems for lead gen and contract acquisition are in place. Now, we’re looking to bring in a seasoned Dispo leader to take our back end to the next level.

________________________________________

, social media, and buyer platforms

• Experience building out dispo operations (flows, documents, systems, etc.)

• Strong written and verbal communication skills

• Detail-oriented and process-driven

• Confident on the phone, text, and
 with investors

 Groups, GHL, Excel/Sheets

title: Real Estate Closer | Convert Warm Leads into Contracts

Description:

We’re seeking a sharp, commission-based Real Estate Closer to join our growing acquisitions team. This is not a cold calling role—you’ll be working exclusively with warm and hot leads already in our pipeline. Your only focus is turning motivated seller conversations into signed wholesale contracts.

We close 4+ deals per month and are scaling. You’ll be walking into a proven system, not trying to build one from scratch. Our team handles lead generation, follow-up, and transaction coordination. We just need a closer who can lock in the deals.

________________________________________

About Us:

We are an experienced real estate investment company working primarily in North Carolina. We specialize in off-market wholesale deals, creative financing, and helping sellers find quick, stress-free solutions. With a reliable marketing engine and a team handling systems, follow-up, and transaction coordination, you’ll be backed by solid infrastructure that allows you to focus on closing.

We consistently provide a steady flow of qualified leads ready for conversion and are committed to building long-term partnerships with our team.

________________________________________

Your Role:

• Speak with warm/hot seller leads by phone

• Build rapport, uncover motivation, and negotiate win-win offers

• Confidently guide the conversation toward a signed contract

• Use our CRM (GHL-based) to track notes and progress

• Submit signed contracts to the Transaction Coordinator for processing

• Follow simple SOPs and scripts

________________________________________

Ideal Candidate:

• 2+ years of experience as a real estate closer (wholesale preferred)

• Strong sales skills with a proven track record of converting leads into contracts

• Confident, clear communicator who can build trust quickly

• Highly organized and able to work independently

• Familiar with tools like GHL, Zoom, and Google Workspace

• Self-motivated and comfortable working commission-only

• U.S. or fluent English speaker who can work during U.S. business hours

________________________________________

Compensation:

• First 2 weeks paid hourly. Commission after that.

• Introductory hourly + Commission – paid per signed contract

• Opportunity to grow with us into a full-time, high-volume closer role

• Bonuses available for high performance and fast-closes

• Part time to full time opportunity

________________________________________

How to Apply:

Please submit the following:

• A short intro explaining your closing experience

• Any KPIs, metrics, or results you’ve hit in past roles

________________________________________

This is a high-opportunity role for someone who’s ready to lock in contracts with confidence. If you're the kind of person who thrives in high-trust conversations and knows how to get deals signed—we want to hear from you.

Media Buyer - Meta & Performance Marketing

We've scaled from just drop shipping to a 30+ person team doing $1M+/month. Our goal? Multi 9-figures. And we're just getting started.



???? Learn more at 
dia



Our Core Values



???? GENIUSES & OWNERS



We partner with builders who think like founders. Not employees waiting to be told what to do—owners who see a problem and fix it.



? ITERATIONS > HOURS



We don't measure success by time at your desk. We measure it by how fast you can test, learn, and improve. Ship it. Learn. Repeat.



???? TRUTH OVER EGO



We prioritize honesty and clarity, even when it's uncomfortable. No politics. No BS. Just data and results.

Strong analytical skills—you live in the data

Experience with $100K+/month spend

Background in creative strategy or copywriting

You've scaled campaigns from test budgets to 6-figure monthly spend

This Role Is NOT For...



We're being direct because we respect your time:



Media buyers who just 'set and forget'

Those who blame the algorithm when things don't work

Anyone who can't explain WHY a campaign is working (or not)

People who aren't comfortable with high stakes and tight targets



Compensation & Benefits



???? Monthly Salary: $1,500/month



Plus:



Monthly performance bonuses tied to company Operating Profit (5-25% of salary)

Work with a team of A-players who actually give a damn

Our Hiring Process

Ready to Apply?

P.P.S.

title: Fire Alarm / Low Voltage Systems Designer ($1280/ month)
description:
Location: Remote



Compensation: $8–$11/hour (based on experience)



About the Role:

We are seeking a skilled Fire Alarm / Security Systems Designer to join our team on a freelance, part-time, or full-time basis. The ideal candidate will have strong technical expertise in fire alarm systems, security systems, CCTV, and access control. Projects will include system design, drafting, and documentation for submittals and installations.



Responsibilities:

- Design fire alarm, security, CCTV, and access control systems in compliance with NFPA, IBC, IFC, and local codes.

- Produce professional drawings and submittal packages using AutoDesk AutoCAD.

- Coordinate system layouts, device locations, riser diagrams, and wiring details.

- Collaborate with project managers, contractors, and AHJs as needed.

- Revise and update designs based on feedback or code requirements.



Qualifications:

- Proven experience designing fire alarm and/or low voltage security systems.

- Proficiency in AutoCAD (required).

- Familiarity with NFPA 72 and other relevant fire/life safety codes.

- Strong attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines.

- Good communication skills and ability to work independently.



Preferred (not required):

- NICET certification in Fire Alarm Systems or equivalent experience.

- Experience preparing full submittal packages for AHJ approval.

- Knowledge of manufacturer-specific equipment (e.g., Fire Lite, DMP, Kidde, Silent Knight, Honeywell, etc.).



To Apply:

- Resume (required).

- Sample projects or work portfolio (preferred).

- Availability (freelance/part
-time).

- Brief summary of relevant experience.

title: Legal Intake VA
description:
Pay: $1,060/month during training (3 months), $1,250/month after

Hours: 40 hrs/week | 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM Chicago Time (night shift for PH)

Location preference: Philippines



We're a busy divorce law firm based in Chicago helping families navigate some of the hardest 
nts of their lives. Our clients are going through separation, custody disputes, and the emotional weight that comes with all of it — and the first voice they hear when they reach out matters enormously. We take that seriously, and we need someone on our team who does too.



As our Legal Intake VA, you'll be the first point of contact for people who are often scared, overwhelmed, or unsure of what to do next. Your job is to answer calls and web inquiries quickly, warmly, and professionally — converting those 
nts of vulnerability into scheduled consultations with our attorneys. You'll work closely with our intake team to keep the calendar organized, the leads followed up on, and the experience seamless from first hello to confirmed appointment.



— Immediate lead response: When a new lead submits a form on our website, you call them back — fast. Speed-to-lead is everything in legal intake, and your quick response is often the difference between a booked consult and a lost client.



— Confident, compassionate phone presence: You'll answer inbound calls with energy and warmth, guiding potential clients through the scheduling process with empathy and professionalism — never robotic, always human.



— Lead follow-up and nurturing: Leads who haven't scheduled yet don't get forgotten. You'll manage consistent outreach through calls and messages, keeping our pipeline warm and moving.



— Appointment management: You'll assist the intake team with confirming, rescheduling, and canceling appointments — keeping attorney calendars accurate and clients properly informed.



— CRM and notes hygiene: Every call, every interaction, every outcome gets logged. You'll keep our intake records clean and up to date so nothing falls through the cracks.



— Schedule coordination support: You'll work alongside our intake team to make sure daily scheduling runs smoothly, flagging any conflicts or gaps that need attention.



- Near-perfect American English — you speak clearly and confidently with little to no accent; our clients must feel at ease immediately

- Minimum 2 years of phone-based work experience — customer service, appointment setting, or similar

- Comfortable making high volumes of outbound calls and handling inbound inquiries without hesitation

- Attention to detail: Include the phrase, "Big journeys begin with small steps" in your cover letter.

- Prior experience in a law firm, medical office, or other sensitive client-facing environment is a strong plus

- Excellent written English for follow-up messaging and CRM notes

- Hardworking, honest, and genuinely caring — you treat every caller like they matter, because they do

- Fast learner who can pick up our processes, scripts, and software quickly

- Comfortable working night shift hours (8:30 AM – 5:30 PM Chicago time)

- Quiet, professional home workspace free of background noise

- Fast, reliable internet connection

- Proficient with Google Workspace and/or Microsoft Office; CRM experience is a plus



This job is not for companies, contractors with multiple clients, or those who have never worked online. Side projects are not allowed.



Training rate is $1,060/month for the first three months, moving to $1,250/month upon successful completion. Pay is sent monthly via Wise.



- 13th month bonus (one month's pay, prorated for partial years)

- Christmas week off (paid)

- Birthday off (paid)

- 7 floating PTO days/year after 3 months

- SSS reimbursement up to $50/mo

- PhilHealth reimbursement up to $50/mo

- Private health insurance reimbursement up to $30/mo

- Discretionary equipment & education reimbursement



You supply your own modern computer and reliable high-speed internet.



Click the application link below. Submit your resume, intro video, and a brief cover letter.



REQUIREMENTS:

- Excellent written and spoken English

- Quiet workspace, fast internet

- Long-term, full-time only



Important: please include the phrase "Big journeys begin with small steps." in your cover letter — applications without this phrase will not be considered.



PAY RATE: $1,060/month during training (3 months), $1,250/month after, paid via Wise.

HOURS PER WEEK: 40 hours.



HOW TO APPLY: Click the link below to apply through our secure portal:





We'll review your application and get back to you within 48 hours.

title: Finance Director
description:
Finance Director — Strategic Finance Leader for Multiple 8–9 Figure Businesses | $3,000/month USD

We Don't Need a Reporter. We Need a Finance Leader Who Helps Us D
 Where to Bet Next.

Most finance people tell you what already happened. You'll tell us what to do next — and own the financial strategy across multiple brands and a SaaS company.

About Rescale Media

We're not just another e-commerce company. We're a brand incubator on a mission to build enduring, value-driven brands that bring meaningful products into families' lives — products rooted in faith, tradition, and timeless values that resist fleeting modern trends.

Our portfolio includes health and beauty brands, plus 
 — our financial intelligence and fractional CFO platform supporting e-commerce founders worldwide.

We've scaled past $2M+/month with a global team, and we're targeting multi 9-figures. The next 12–24 months will be defined by capital allocation, margin expansion, and disciplined scaling decisions — and that's exactly where this role lives.

???? Learn more at 
dia

Our Core Values

???? GENIUSES & OWNERS We partner with builders who think like founders. Not finance people waiting for instructions — operators who see a margin problem, build the model, and walk into the CEO's office with a recommendation.

? ITERATIONS > HOURS We don't measure success by time at your desk. We measure it by how fast you can pressure-test a hypothesis, refine the forecast, and turn data into a better decision. Build it. Stress-test it. Improve it. Repeat.

???? TRUTH OVER EGO We prioritize honesty and clarity, even when it's uncomfortable. If a brand is unprofitable, you say it. If a campaign is bleeding cash, you flag it. If leadership is wrong, you push back with data. No politics. No spin. Just truth.

The Role

This is not a traditional finance reporting role. This is a builder role for someone who wants to operate as a Fractional CFO across an entire portfolio of businesses.

You will:

Sit at the right hand of the CEO as a strategic thought partner

Own financial strategy across multiple brands and 

Drive decisions on capital allocation, scaling, pricing, and profitability

Manage and develop the Finance Manager (your direct report)

Build the financial backbone that allows us to scale to multi 9-figures without losing control of the numbers

If you've been the smartest finance person in every room and you're tired of presenting reports to leaders who don't act on them — this is your seat.

What You'll Own

Strategic Finance & FP&A

Own financial strategy across all business units (multiple DTC brands + SaaS)

Build and maintain advanced financial models — actuals reporting, forecasting, scenario planning, cash flow projections

Translate complex financial data into clear, actionable decisions for the CEO and leadership

Prepare investor-ready materials and supporting analysis as needed

Unit Economics & Performance

Analyze CAC, LTV, contribution margin, operating margin, and blended ROAS across brands

Deliver weekly and monthly performance insights that drive real decisions

Identify margin leaks, inefficient spend, and cost optimization opportunities before they become problems

Capital & Cash Flow

Manage cash flow planning across multiple entities

Drive capital allocation decisions — where to invest, where to cut, where to scale

Implement financial reporting structures that give leadership a real-time view of the business

Leadership & Operations

Manage and develop the Finance Manager — ensuring strong execution of accounting operations and reporting

Support pricing, margin, and operational efficiency initiatives across departments

Partner with department leads to align financial reality with operational decisions

What We're Looking For

5–10+ years in finance with proven leadership experience

Strong e-commerce / DTC experience — non-negotiable. You understand how this industry actually makes money.

Deep expertise in financial modeling and forecasting — not just maintaining models, but building them from scratch

Strong grasp of unit economics and the growth levers that move the business

Direct experience working with founders or operators — not just middle management

Advanced Excel / Google Sheets skills — modeling, scenario analysis, automation

Operator mindset — you don't just analyze, you make calls

Sharp communicator — you can take a 50-line P&L and make a CEO understand the one thing that matters in 30 seconds

Bonus Points

Experience operating in a Fractional CFO capacity

Experience managing multiple entities, brands, or business units

SaaS finance experience alongside DTC

Experience managing and developing junior finance talent

Comfort with 
 or similar e-commerce financial intelligence tools

Resonance with our mission to build faith-led, traditional, value-driven brands

This Role Is NOT For:

People who want to be told what to do

Reporters who track the past but can't predict the future

Finance people who hide behind spreadsheets instead of making recommendations

Anyone who needs ego massaging or office politics

People uncomfortable with founder-level directness or pushing back when leadership is wrong

Anyone looking for a clock-in, clock-out role

People who don't resonate with our mission of building traditional, faith-led, value-driven brands

If you read those bullets and felt defensive, this isn't the role for you. Move on. If you read them and thought "finally, a place that gets it" — keep reading.

Compensation & Benefits

Base Salary: $3,000 USD/month (full-time)

Performance-Based Bonuses:

Individual Monthly Bonus: Up to 25% of monthly salary based on company Operating Profit %, with double bonus % as a Department Lead (up to 50%)

Quarterly Profit Pool: Equal share of the Employee Profit Pool (2.0%–7.5% of operating profit, scaling with monthly gross sales)

Long-term profit and/or equity incentives for the right person

Other Benefits:

Fully remote

European team orientation

Direct line to the CEO — your work moves the business

Front-row seat to scaling multiple 8–9 figure brands plus a SaaS platform

Our Hiring Process

We don't do gut-feel hiring. We do standards and data.

Application — Submit through the form below

Screening / Task Test — A short, role-specific exercise to see how you actually think

Founder Interview — Direct conversation with the CEO

Paid Trial Project — Real work, real compensation, real signal

Ready to Apply?

[ 
 ]

Complete the application form above. Be thorough — this is your first impression.

Please attach your resume.

P.S. The best finance leaders aren't reporting on the past — they're shaping the future. If you've ever sat in a leadership meeting and thought "I could be running the financial strategy here" — this is your seat.

P.P.S. We're not the biggest, but we're building something that matters. If a mission to build faith-led, traditional, value-driven brands resonates with you — you've found your team.

title: Cold Caller & Lead Qualifier (Mortgage Brokerage)
description:
About Us:

We are a growing mortgage brokerage specializing in funding for real estate investors. We help clients secure financing for Fix & Flip, DSCR, and New Construction projects. We’re looking for a motivated and reliable Virtual Assistant to join our team and focus on cold calling, qualifying leads, and setting appointments.



Responsibilities:

Make outbound calls to prospective real estate investors from provided lead lists

Qualify leads by asking pre-set questions (investment experience, current projects, financing needs, etc.)

Follow scripts while being able to adapt and engage naturally in conversation

Book qualified appointments for our loan officers

Enter notes, call outcomes, and lead details into our CRM (GoHighLevel experience is a plus)

Track daily activity (calls made, contacts reached, appointments set)

Provide consistent communication and updates to the team



Requirements:

Strong English communication skills (clear, confident phone presence)

Prior experience in cold calling, appointment setting, or sales (mortgage, lending, or real estate background preferred)

Ability to handle objections and stay persistent

Comfortable working with CRMs, dialers, and basic lead management tools

Reliable internet connection, computer, and quiet work environment

Self-motivated and organized with strong attention to detail



Preferred Skills:

Previous experience in the mortgage industry (MUST HAVE) – mortgage cold calling, loan coordination, appointment setting, or similar

Familiarity with mortgage loan programs (Fix & Flip, DSCR, New Construction)

GoHighLevel CRM experience

Appointment setting for financial or real estate services



Compensation:

Hourly rate: $4 – $8/hr (depending on experience)

Bonus structure available for each qualified appointment set



Schedule:

Full-time available (minimum 40 hours/week)



How to Apply:

Please send your application with the following:

A short introduction about yourself and your experience in cold calling/appointment setting.

A voice recording (60 seconds or less) introducing yourself.

title: Permitting Coordinator (Architecture / Residential Projects)
description:
Position Summary:

We are seeking a highly organized and proactive architect to oversee the permit application process for residential projects in California. This role will involve coordinating with local building departments, ensuring compliance with regulations, and expediting permit approvals to keep projects moving efficiently. The ideal candidate will work closely with the design department to prepare required documentation, monitor project timelines, and communicate confidently with city officials, consultants, and internal teams.



Key Responsibilities:

- Manage the permit application process for residential projects, ensuring all submissions meet state and local regulatory requirements.

- Work closely with the design department to gather necessary plans, reports, and specifications for permit submissions.

- Liaise with city officials, building departments, and third-party consultants to facilitate approvals and resolve any permitting issues.

- Track and monitor permit timelines, providing regular updates to internal stakeholders to maintain efficient progress.

- Identify opportunities to expedite permit approvals by proactively addressing compliance requirements and coordinating necessary revisions.

- Ensure adherence to California building codes, zoning laws, and environmental regulations in all permit applications.

- Maintain organized documentation for permits, approvals, and project correspondence.

- Stay informed of changes in local permitting policies that may affect project timelines and requirements.



Qualifications:

- Bachelor’s degree in Architecture, Construction Management, Urban Planning.

- Licensed Architect (preferred) or relevant experience in permit coordination for residential projects.

- Strong knowledge of California building codes, zoning regulations, and permit processes.

- Experience working with city agencies, planning boards, and regulatory bodies.

- Exceptional organizational skills and the ability to track multiple projects efficiently.

- Highly confident communicator with strong negotiation and problem-solving abilities.

- Proficiency in AutoCAD, Revit, and other architectural documentation tools.

- Familiarity with permit expediting strategies and local jurisdiction requirements.



Send your CV and Portfolio, make sure it is accessible. Goodluck!

title: Cold Caller / Outbound Sales Development Representative
description:
About TaskOn eCommerce

TaskOn eCommerce is a Klaviyo Master Platinum Partner and retention marketing agency. We help 7–8 figure DTC brands on Shopify maximize revenue through
, SMS, loyalty, and owned-channel marketing. We also manage Amazon Vendor Central (1P) for select clients. Our team works with brands in health & wellness, pet supplies, beauty, and sports/outdoor verticals.



About the Role

We’re looking for a motivated and professional Cold Caller to join our team. Your primary responsibility is to reach out to DTC brand founders, eCommerce directors, and Amazon decision-makers on behalf of TaskOn eCommerce and book qualified discovery calls with our leadership team.



This is a high-activity outbound role. You will be provided with background on TaskOn eCommerce’s services, value proposition, and target client profile to guide your conversations. Scripts and call frameworks will be developed collaboratively over time. Your job is to make contact, build rapport quickly, communicate what we do clearly, and secure a booked discovery call.



Please note: this role requires working Pacific Standard Time (PST) hours. Candidates who are unable to work PST hours will not be considered.



What You’ll Be Doing

• Making outbound calls to DTC brand founders and key decision-makers

• Following provided call frameworks while adapting naturally to the conversation

• Qualifying prospects based on criteria provided by leadership

• Booking discovery calls directly onto the calendar of our President & Founder

• Logging all activity, call outcomes, and notes in our CRM

• Following up with prospects via phone and
 as needed

• Providing daily or weekly activity summaries to leadership



What We’re Looking For

• 3+ years with demonstrated results in cold calling and booking meetings - this is a firm requirement

• Fluent English speaker with clear, professional phone communication skills

• Proven track record of booking qualified meetings with business owners and decision-makers

• Comfortable speaking with founders and senior-level contacts

• Highly organized, self-motivated, and accountable

• Coachable - you follow provided frameworks while bringing your own energy and initiative

• Reliable internet connection and a quiet, professional calling environment

• Familiarity with CRM tools and call tracking is a plus

• Knowledge of eCommerce, Shopify, and digital marketing is a strong plus 



What to Expect in the First 90 Days

This role comes with clearly defined 30, 60, and 90-day goals that will be shared upon hire. These are firm targets, and we believe the right person will see them as a clear roadmap for success rather than a hurdle.



What Success Looks Like

• A consistent volume of outbound calls made daily

• Qualified discovery calls booked onto leadership’s calendar

• Clean, accurate logging of all call activity

• Achieving the target goals set



Compensation

This role offers a base salary plus a two-tier commission structure. You will earn a commission for each completed discovery call that meets our qualification criteria, and a larger commission bonus when a prospect converts into a paying client. This structure rewards both consistent activity and results.



To Apply

Please submit your resume along with a short voice recording (1–2 minutes) introducing yourself and explaining why you’d be a great fit for this role. Applications without a voice recording will not be considered.

title: Full-Time HVAC Dispatcher/ Phone representative (Housecall Pro REQUIRED- US Company))
description:

title: Project Coordinator
description:
Role: Project Coordinator (Team Coordinator)

Hours: Full Time, Remote, EST Hours

Us:  A growing restoration company



The Project Coordinator supports the successful execution of restoration projects by managing scheduling, customer communication, vendor coordination and administrative processes.  This role ensures that estimators and project managers are supported with accurate information, organized workflows, and timely communication so that projects run smoothly from start to finish.



KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES

Customer Service & Communication 

? Serve as the primary point of contact for customers by providing updates, scheduling appointments, and ensuring a high level of customer satisfaction throughout the project lifecycle. 

Scheduling & Project Coordination 

? Coordinate with Estimators and Project Managers to schedule inspections, job starts, and subcontractor activities to ensure projects stay on track and timelines are met. 

Vendor & Subcontractor Coordination 

? Manage vendor communication, obtain quotes, coordinate deliveries, and ensure subcontractors meet project requirements and timelines. 

Billing & Documentation Support 

? Prepare, review, and process invoices, work orders, and purchase orders while coordinating with accounting to ensure accuracy and timeliness. 

Administrative Support 

? Provide ongoing administrative assistance to Project Managers and Estimators, including documentation, reporting, and project closeout tasks. 

Process Improvement 

? Identify opportunities to streamline administrative workflows and improve communication between teams to increase efficiency and effectiveness. 



QUALIFICATIONS

Required:

? 3+ years of administrative experience (restoration or construction preferred)

? Strong organizational and time management skills

? Excellent written and verbal communication skills

? Proficiency in Google Workspace and project management software

? Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment

? High attention to detail and accuracy

Preferred:

? Experience in restoration or construction industry

? Familiarity with project coordination workflows

? Experience working with vendors and subcontractors

? Willingness to learn and adapt to new systems and technologies

Bonus if you have worked with EOS (Entrepreneurial Operating Systems) companies previously.



To apply, please reply with a link to your resume and your desired hourly rate of pay.  Thanks!

title: Part-Time Real Estate Cold Caller / Inside Sales Agent (ISA)
description:
Remote | Part-Time | U.S. Real Estate



We are a growing real estate business based in Washington State (USA) looking for a reliable and confident Inside Sales Agent (ISA) to help us connect with homeowners and set appointments.



This role is focused on outbound calling. You will be speaking with property owners, identifying opportunities, and setting appointments for our real estate agent.



We are building a long-term system and are looking for someone who is professional, consistent, and comfortable speaking with people on the phone.



What You’ll Be Doing

Calling homeowners from prepared lead lists

Following a structured call approach (not sounding scripted)

Identifying homeowners interested in selling

Setting appointments for our real estate agent

Recording clear and accurate notes in the CRM

Submitting a daily call report



???? You are not responsible for closing deals — your role is to start conversations and set appointments.



Schedule



Part-time to start.



Must be available for daytime to evening shifts (PST)

Monday – Friday, with flexibility to work weekends as needed



Schedule will be assigned based on business needs and performance.



Additional hours may be available as the team grows.



What We’re Looking For

Experience with cold calling or inside sales

Clear and neutral English speaking voice

Confident and conversational on the phone

Comfortable handling objections

Ability to follow a structure while sounding natural

Strong attention to detail when taking notes

Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace

Familiarity with Washington State real estate market is strongly preferred



???? Real estate experience is a plus, but not required.



Compensation

Competitive hourly pay

Performance bonuses for appointments set

Opportunity for long-term work with a growing team



Specific compensation details will be discussed during the interview process.



Tools You’ll Be Using

CRM system (BoldTrail)

Dialer system

Prepared call lists and scripts

Structured reporting system



???? We provide the systems — you bring the communication and consistency.



How to Apply



To apply, please include:



1?? A short voice recording introducing yourself

2?? Your experience with cold calling or sales

3?? Your internet speed test result

4?? The word “I am the one” in the subject line



???? Applications without a voice recording will not be considered.



Who We Are



We are a relationship-focused real estate business that values professionalism, integrity, and clear systems.



We believe great conversations lead to great opportunities, and we are building a team that helps homeowners make smart decisions about their property.

title: Quantity Takeoff Estimator
description:
US. Central Time 8-5 M-F Full time position



Quantity Takeoff / Junior Estimator

(Masonry, Concrete & Hardscape)



Position Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented Quantity Takeoff Specialist to support our estimating team

in a growing masonry, fencing, concrete, and hardscape company. This role is designed as a

development position, with the goal of training the right candidate into a full-time

Commercial Estimator.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone who wants to build a long-term career in

construction estimating and project management within the U.S. commercial construction

industry.



Key Responsibilities

• Perform quantity takeoffs from plans and specifications for masonry, fencing,

hardscape, and concrete work

• Interpret architectural, civil, and structural drawings

• Apply U.S. construction standards, building codes, and specifications

• Measure materials using U.S. units (SF, LF, CY, tons, etc.)

• Break down concrete scopes including yardage, rebar quantities, and formwork

• Organize quantities into estimating spreadsheets and templates

• Assist with bid preparation, scope reviews, and proposals

• Maintain organized documentation of all takeoffs



Growth Path

This role is intended to develop into a Commercial Estimator position, including building

full project estimates, pricing labor and materials, understanding code requirements,

managing bids, and interacting with contractors and clients.



Qualifications

• Required:

• Strong attention to detail

• Ability to read construction drawings

• Familiarity with U.S. construction units

• Basic understanding of U.S. building codes

• Proficiency in Excel



• Strong organizational skills

• Preferred:

• Experience with takeoff software (PlanSwift, Bluebeam, OST)

• Experience with concrete and rebar takeoffs

• Background in masonry or site work

• Estimating or project coordination experience



What We’re Looking For

• Desire to grow into a commercial estimator role

• Comfort working with detailed plans and specs

• Strong problem-solving mindset

• Reliable and deadline-driven

• Interest in a long-term construction career



Why This Role is Different

This is not just a takeoff position. We are hiring and training someone to become a key

estimator capable of understanding full project scope, including code requirements and

constructability.

title: Landscape Architect
description:
Position Summary

We are seeking a talented and detail-oriented Landscape Architect to join our remote design team. This role is responsible for developing high-quality landscape plans for residential projects in California while ensuring seamless coordination with our design group. The ideal candidate will have expertise in landscape design, site planning, and sustainability, with strong communication and collaboration skills for remote teamwork.



Key Responsibilities

- Design landscape plans for residential projects in California, ensuring aesthetic appeal and functionality.

- Create planting plans, hardscape layouts, irrigation designs, and outdoor living concepts tailored to client needs.

- Research and apply California-specific zoning, environmental, and planting regulations to design solutions.

- Develop technical drawings, site plans, and 3D renderings using industry-standard software.

- Coordinate closely with the design team, project managers, and consultants to align landscaping with overall project vision.

- Assist in concept presentations, material specifications, and design revisions based on client feedback.

- Stay informed on sustainable landscape practices, native plant selections, and water-efficient designs.

- Manage multiple projects while ensuring timely delivery of high-quality landscape plans.



Qualifications

- Bachelor’s degree in Landscape Architecture, Architecture, or a related field.

- Proven experience in residential landscape design and remote collaboration.

- Proficiency in AutoCAD, SketchUp, Lumion, Adobe Creative Suite, and other landscape design tools.

- Strong understanding of California climate, planting guidelines, and sustainability principles.

- Excellent communication and coordination skills for working remotely with a U.S.-based team.

- Ability to manage time effectively and deliver quality work within project deadlines.

- Knowledge of irrigation systems, hardscape materials, and outdoor lighting is a plus.





Kindly send your CV/Resume and Portfolio and make sure it is accessible. Goodluck!

ets daily and weekly operational targets

mbers and stakeholders

title: UK Company Hiring: US Cold Callers
description:
Hiring  Cold callers to Help Build Local Business In the USA

 Base pay $600 per month (after two week training) plus commissions OTE each month $1000-$1500

Company: Start Over Movement Holdings Ltd (Founded by Sir Marco Robinson)

Location: Remote - 9:00 PM - 6:00 AM Philippines Time (8:00 AM - 5:00 PM Mississippi Central Time)

Type: Full-time Independent Contractor

---

The Story Behind Start Over

Sir Marco Robinson is a UK entrepreneur, author, and TV personality who has spent decades helping people rebuild their lives and businesses. He founded Start Over Movement with a simple idea: local business owners should support each other, not compete for the same scraps.

Every Start Over chapter is a community of local business owners who:

Refer clients to each other, generating real revenue

Learn marketing and sales directly from Sir Marco

Get a dedicated commission-only closer assigned to their business

Meet monthly with an exclusive group (only one business per industry per chapter)

It is not networking. It is a referral engine with a mentor attached. Members pay £1,500 (UK) or $1,500 (US and Canada) per year to be part of it. And the model works - we already have active chapters in Laguna Beach, Tampa Bay, New York, London and Toronto. Mississippi is next.

---

Why We Need You

We are looking for 3 cold callers each week to join the team now. We need in total another 180 cold callers within 3 months so this team and the level of recruitment will grow quickly to help launch and fill  up a weekly webinar  the Mississippi chapter. This is part of a broader buildout of 100+ cold callers across all our territories.



The business owners we call are not hostile. They are overwhelmed, under-marketed, and tired of networking groups that do not deliver. When you explain what Start Over actually does - referrals, mentorship, exclusive local representation - the good ones lean in.

closers then to attend a webinar designed to close them for us





The 14-Day Trial

We do not rush this. Your first 14 days are a paid trial:

$15/day base, paid daily via Wise (for verified work days)

Full commission on every sale you close during the trial

Day 1: Onboarding, script training, shadowing, pass the roleplay by end of day

Day 2 onwards: Full production with daily minimums

Live coaching from the Cold Caller Manager throughout

Roleplay check must be passed on Day 1 before going on real calls Day 2

---

What Makes This Role Different

1. You are not hustling a bad offer. Start Over is real. Sir Marco is real. Members are paying, showing up, and referring each other. You are selling something that delivers.

2. You are part of a real team. We are building out 100+ closers across all territories, but we do it properly. You are not one of thousands dialling into a chaotic floor. You get proper coaching, real territory, clear structure, and a Cold Caller Manager who knows your name and your numbers.

3. You have a growth path. Mississippi is the start. As we launch new chapters, top performers get first pick. Build your book with us and grow as we grow.

4. You get real support. Cold Caller Manager in twice-weekly coaching sessions. Direct line to Marco Fernandes (Sales Director, 39 years of sales experience). Proven script refined across thousands of calls. Calendar booking, leads, and territory handed to you.

---

The Daily Reality

Let us be honest about what the day looks like:

9:00 PM - 6:00 AM Philippines Time (8:00 AM - 5:00 PM Mississippi Central Time) - yes, graveyard

Monday to Friday, five nights a week

300 dials or 120 connects minimum per day from Day 2 (whichever you hit first)

10+ event bookings per day (prospects committed to attend the next Start Over event), OR 1+ direct sale, OR 15+ strong Zoom follow-ups booked

Log every call in real time on the shared tracker

End-of-day report to the Cold Caller Manager

Twice weekly meetings (Tuesdays and Fridays typically)

It is not light work. Cold calling is hard. Graveyard is harder. But it is clear work with clear outcomes, and for the right person it pays better than almost anything else a Filipino remote worker can do.

---

We Are Looking For Someone Who

Must have:

2+ years phone sales or closing experience

OnlineJobs.ph ID Proof 60+ verified on your profile

NBI clearance (or willingness to obtain within 2 weeks of hire)

Strong English, clear accent, professional tone

Real experience closing on the call (not just setting appointments)

Can commit to 9:00 PM - 6:00 AM PHT graveyard shift, Monday to Friday

Own Zoom Pro account

Reliable internet (25+ Mbps - speed test screenshot required with application), quiet space, good headset

Will thrive if you:

Have sold coaching, consulting, BNI, networking memberships, or community products before

Care about what you sell, not just about commission

Take coaching well

Can handle "no" all day without losing energy

Want a long-term role, not a quick gig

---

Honest Caveats

The first chapter in every territory is the hardest. List building, market education, all of it.

Your first week, you may not close. That is normal. Week 2 is where the trial gate lives.

Mississippi is a smaller market than our other chapters. That means you can cover the whole state, but you will also work the list harder.

Overnight shifts are tough. Please do not apply if you cannot genuinely commit to the schedule long-term.

---

Verification and Accountability

We use:

TimeProof (you install on your machine, screenshots and activity tracking)

GoHighLevel / Ringover (dial tracking, call recordings, connect verification)

Shared call tracker (real-time logging)

Random call audits (5+ recorded calls per contractor per day reviewed)

This is not surveillance for its own sake. It is so that:

We know you are working the hours you claim

You are protected if we ever question your output - the data has your back

Coaching can be real, because we can listen to your actual calls

We pay for work verified by these systems. Discrepancies get paid only to the verified level. Clear and fair.

Zero tolerance for fraud: Fabricated connects, mouse jigglers or other activity simulators, fake call logs, or dials to deliberately invalid numbers = immediate termination. Pay for fabricated work will be withheld or clawed back. This is the only thing we are strict about. Real closers never have an issue with this.

---

Termination

Independent contractor. Either party can end the agreement at any time, with or without cause, without notice. We pay for all hours worked and commissions earned up to that point.

---

Group Interview - MONDAY

We are hosting a group interview tomorrow (Monday) at 7pm Manila time (12pm UK, 7am Mississippi).





Zoom link:





 









This is how we meet candidates at scale and make fast decisions. Come ready with:

Webcam and microphone working

Professional background and attire

Ready to do a short live roleplay on a cold call

Questions about the role

If you cannot attend live, send your application anyway and we will include you in the next round.

---

How To Apply

Message us through OnlineJobs.ph with:

Name and location

Internet speed test screenshot (from speedtest.net - must show 25+ Mbps)

ID Proof score (60+ required) and NBI clearance status



Confirmation that you can work 9:00 PM - 6:00 AM PHT graveyard shift consistently

No voice recording = we cannot review you. We hire on voice, not paper, for phone sales roles.

---

Start Over Movement. Building real communities. Paying real money. Looking for Three STRONG cold calllers NOW for Mississippi

We look forward to meeting you tomorrow.

title: Healthcare Technical Support
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:


/ansiblehealth/17b2a6df-a72d-480e-8c91-daa3830ac000



What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

As a Technical Product Support Specialist, your primary responsibility is to provide patients with exceptional technical support for our products and services. You will daily engage non-tech-savvy patients on technical phone calls. Most of our patients are older adults located in the USA. These calls involve guiding patients through using their Android tablet and verifying it in the backend through Samsung Knox, ensuring that RPM devices are connected and transmitting accurate data, and assisting them in joining their appointments through our tablet via Zoom. Occasionally, patients may require additional help during their time in our program. In some instances, verifying data through Azure (Device Analytics and CosmosDB) may be necessary.



Furthermore, you will provide technical support to our clinical team, and ensure they are prepared for their virtual appointments. They may also require assistance using Athenahealth, such as modifying visits and checking in/out. You will handle general general technical support duties, which demand a solid understanding of our tech stack and products (i.e., Android Tablet, Zoo
etings and phone, Google Workspace). You should be capable of providing basic troubleshooting steps and assistance to remove obstacles for clinicians/support staff in their work, and are expected to be able to have on-call availability for urgent situations where patient care is affected.



Collaborating closely with our Engineering Team is also an essential aspect of your role to effectively escalate issues requiring broader coordination and resolution. GitLab and Azure DevOps are the primary ticketing systems used for filing issues and requests. Continuous follow-ups and monitoring are expected until the issue is resolved end-to-end. In addition, you are expected to be able to present and take notes on meetings about issues encountered in Tech.



The ideal candidate should be comfortable navigating ambiguity due to the ever-evolving nature of our startup. To excel in this role, excellent problem-solving, communication, and organizational skills are essential.



A Technical Product Support Specialist is expected to:

• Maintain a reliable, high-speed internet connection

• Handle Tech Support requests filed by clinicians or patients from end to end

• Manage and organize various project tools (e.g., GitLab, AzureDevOps, spreadsheets, bug trackers, documents)

• Conduct Tech Calls for non-tech-savvy patients (mainly older adults) in the USA

• Guide patients through Android tablet usage and confirm settings through Samsung Knox

• Work with RPM devices to ensure they are connected and transmitting accurate data

• Collaborate with Engineering Team and Program Managers to escalate and resolve issues

• Assist clinicians and company support staff with technical issues

• Run queries and check analytics using Azure’s CosmosDB or Device Analytics

• Become extremely familiar with our proprietary app, tablets, RPM devices, and backend systems.

• Support clinical team with equipment setup and checking applied security policies

• Present and take notes on Technical meetings, specifically on issues encountered and plans to resolve them.



Qualifications and Experience

• Prior experience in a customer support role, such as Technical Support Specialist or Support Engineer

• Expertise in Android Tablets, Samsung Knox Suite, and Knox Manage

• Proficiency in managing Zoo
etings and Zoom Phone

• Familiarity with medical devices (e.g., Blood Pressure monitor, Pulse Oximeter)

• Knowledge of Athenahealth preferred

• Experience using issue tracking software and remote support tools (e.g., Gitlab, Azure DevOps) is a plus

• Understanding of technology systems, particularly Google Workspace

• Positive, enthusiastic attitude and excellent English language skills (C2 level)

• Skilled in technical debugging techniques (e.g., analyzing metrics and logs on Azure)

• Strong written and verbal communication, problem-solving skills, and detail-oriented

• Ability to manage multiple priorities effectively

• Healthcare setting experience advantageous



Type of Role

Full-time: 40 hours a week (with a possible shifting schedule including weekends)

Benefits: Flexible PTO and HMO coverage.

Level: Mid-Career, 3-5 years relevant work experience

Location: Remote

title: General Program Manager
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:


/ansiblehealth/4b610b44-ac59-43da-a514-ca69e2cce9c3



We are seeking a dynamic and talented General Program Manager to join our team. This role is perfect for a highly intelligent, motivated individual who is passionate about leveraging technology to make a difference in healthcare.



What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

We're seeking a super smart, hungry, and resilient individual who is passionate about technology and healthcare to join us as a Program Manager. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment and brings a strong organizational mindset to complex projects. You will be responsible for overseeing multiple complex programs across various departments to ensure successful delivery within scope, timeline, and budget. This role requires a self-motivated individual who can work independently and adapt to the dynamic nature of a growing startup in the healthcare technology space.



Responsibilities

- Impact the strategy and execution for AnsibleHealth's general operations, clinical operations, and technology initiatives, working closely with cross-functional teams.

- Work independently on projects through the full life cycle, overseeing clinical, operational, and technology aspects.

- Develop and manage program plans, schedules, budgets, and resource allocation to ensure successful delivery.

- Define program scope, objectives, and success criteria in collaboration with stakeholders and cross-functional teams.

- Establish program governance structures and processes, including risk management, issue management, and change management.

- Monitor and report on program progress, status, and performance to stakeholders and executive leadership.

- Ensure effective communication and alignment with stakeholders at all levels.

- Continuously identify and mitigate program risks and issues.

- Facilitate program-related meetings and workshops.

- Develop and maintain strong relationships with stakeholders to drive program success and identify growth opportunities.

- Bring a continuous improvement mindset to our operations to ensure we're ready to scale.

- Anticipate issues, propose solutions, manage dependencies, and identify areas for tool/process improvement.

- Collaborate with and steer internal teams, including Marketing, Engineering, Program Management, and Clinical Operations.



The ideal candidate should be comfortable to be in a constantly changing role with a high level of ambiguity - as we are a fast-moving startup operating in an ever-evolving environment. He or she should have excellent analytical, communication and management skills to succeed in this role.



Education

Bachelor's degree from one of the top five universities in the Philippines.



Required Qualifications and competencies

- Fresh graduates are welcome to apply; 0-5 years of relevant work experience is an advantage but not required.

- Demonstrated passion for technology and ability to quickly adapt to new digital tools and platforms.

- Exceptional organizational skills.

- Resilient mindset with the ability to bounce back from challenges and thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.

- Keen interest in healthcare and improving patient outcomes; prior healthcare experience is a plus but not mandatory.

- High intellectual curiosity and a hunger for personal and professional development.

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to think critically and creatively.

- Proficiency in Google Workspace and willingness to learn new software tools as needed.

- Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and as part of a team.

- Adaptable and comfortable with ambiguity in a startup environment.

- Eagerness to take on leadership roles and initiative to drive projects forward.

- Ability to influence and collaborate with stakeholders at all levels.

- Strong attention to detail and commitment to delivering high-quality work.

- Enthusiasm for continuous learning and process improvement.



The ideal candidate is a highly intelligent, motivated individual who is passionate about leveraging technology to make a difference in healthcare. We're looking for someone who can bring fresh perspectives, adapt quickly to new challenges, and contribute to our innovative and fast-paced work environment.



Job Details

Full-time: Full-time: 40 hours a week

Benefits: Flexible PTO and HMO coverage

Location: Remote (offshore, and willing to work US hours - EST/EDT)

title: # Part-Time Marketing VA — Meta Ads & Client Success Manager
description:
## About DVRG



DVRG is a Tacoma-based marketing agency that produces high-impact video content, Meta ad creative, and lead generation systems for service-based businesses — contractors, remodelers, painters, and home service operators across the Pacific Northwest.



We run lean, move fast, and hold our work to a high standard. We're not a big agency with layers of management. We're a focused operation that takes pride in results and creative quality.



We're looking for a part-time VA to take the operational side of our campaigns off the founder's plate so he can stay in sales and strategy.



---



## The Role



This is a **10-hour per week, part-time remote position** — roughly 3 hours on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday. Work is async-friendly, but we ask for at least a 1-hour overlap with Pacific Standard Time (PST) during morning hours for a weekly check-in.



You'll work directly with the founder across three core areas:



**1. Campaign Management & Reporting**

- Pull weekly Meta Ads performance reports across 6 active client accounts

- Monitor campaigns for underperformance (high CPL, low CTR, ad fatigue)

- Flag issues and log updates into our internal Slack tracker

- Upload approved creative and build out ad sets in Meta Business Suite

- Build and QA Meta Instant Forms



**2. Client Success Support**

- Send weekly performance recap messages to clients via Slack

- Follow up on missing assets, deliverables, or pending approvals

- Keep client communication organized and nothing falling through the cracks



**3. Operations & Admin**

- Organize Google Drive folders by client and date

- Format finalized ad scripts into our approved document template

- Maintain our ad swipe file with hooks and winning creative

- Research competitor ads via Meta Ad Library for client niches

- Support Zapier automation monitoring (flag broken zaps)



---



## You're a Fit If You:



- Have 2+ years of experience in a digital marketing agency or as a marketing VA

- Have hands-on experience inside Meta Business Suite / Ads Manager on real client accounts

- Know what CPL, CTR, ROAS, and ad frequency mean — no explanation needed

- Have worked with US-based clients before

- Are comfortable in Slack, Google Drive, and async communication

- Are detail-oriented, proactive, and flag problems before they become issues

- Can work independently without hand-holding on standard tasks



---



## Tools We Use



Meta Business Suite · Slack · Google Drive · Google Docs · Zapier · Notion



---



## What This Is NOT



- This is not a content creation or social media scheduling role

- This is not an entry-level position — we need someone who can hit the ground running

- This is not a full-time role (for now) — but there is room to grow as we scale



---



## Compensation & Hours



- **Rate:** $8 USD/hr

- **Hours:** ~10 hrs/week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday — ~3 hrs each)

- **Schedule:** Async with 1-hour PST overlap window preferred

- **Start:** ASAP



---



## How to Apply



Send a message with:



1. Your resume or a link to your OnlineJobs profile

2. A 2–3 sentence summary of your Meta Ads experience (be specific — what did you manage, what was the monthly ad spend, how many accounts?)

3. Any tools from our stack you've used before

4. Your availability and timezone



**Applications without Meta Ads experience will not be considered.**



We respond to every qualified application within 48 hours.

Qualifications

Strong knowledge of drafting standards, construction methods, and technical terminology.

Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills.

Strong communication and teamwork abilities.

Preferred Skills

Fully Remote
