title: AI Creative Specialist
description:
TO APPLY

If this opportunity feels like the perfect fit for you and you meet the job qualifications, we invite you to learn more about our HiEnd Accents team in the Philippines and submit your application here:


com/appzq4iqeIREQRVyo/pagWFW91EJQpT08fw/form NOTE: Change the word "(dot)" to an actual dot.



Applications submitted through onlinejobs.ph will not be reviewed.



JOB DESCRIPTION



We're looking for a full-time AI Creative Specialist to lead the creation of AI-generated product imagery, lifestyle scenes, and short-form video for our brands, including HiEnd Accents and Paseo Road. You'll take our products from a flat studio shot to fully-realized lifestyle photography and video creative used across our website, paid social ads,
 campaigns, and Pinterest. You'll own the full pipeline: writing prompts, generating images and videos with tools like Gemini (Nano Banana Pro), Midjourney, Kling, and Veo, finishing them in Photoshop/Canva if needed, and assembling them into finished ad creatives.



We are looking for a long-term tea
mber who will stay and grow at our company. We offer competitive compensation and a good work environment.





WHAT YOU'LL DO



- Generate photorealistic lifestyle product photography using AI image tools (Gemini / Nano Banana Pro, Midjourney, or similar), placing our bedding, pillows, rugs, and home textiles into believable room scenes, on-model shots, and editorial-style compositions



- Animate hero images into short-form video using Kling, Veo 3/3.1, or comparable image-to-video tools for use in Meta ads, Pinterest, TikTok, and on-site PDP video



- Write, test, and continuously refine your own prompts, building reusable prompt templates and a library of looks that maintain visual consistency across each brand



- Build finished static and video ad creatives (Meta, Pinterest, Google) by combining AI-generated imagery with on-brand text overlays, logos, and sizing variants



- Finish and retouch every AI output in Photoshop, Canva, and/or Lightroom, fixing hands, edges, lighting, fabric texture, and any AI artifacts before delivery so nothing leaves your hands looking AI-generated



- Quality-check every output against the actual product reference photos before anything goes out the door, comparing print patterns, color, scale, flange widths, fabric texture, stitching, and proportions to the real product side by side, and regenerating or retouching until the match is exact



- Maintain brand consistency across every output, staying within each brand's color palette, lighting style, prop language, and overall aesthetic (HiEnd Accents vs. Paseo Road vs. our other brands)



- Iterate quickly on feedback from the marketing and creative teams, delivering multiple variations and revisions per brief



- Organize and tag final assets in our shared library (Google Drive, Airtable, Notion) so the rest of the team can find and reuse them



- Track what's working, which creatives are performing on ads, which prompts are producing the best output, and feed those learnings into the next round



- Stay current on new AI image and video tools, model updates, and prompting techniques, and bring promising ones to the team





WHO YOU ARE



- Significant prior experience generating photorealistic product or lifestyle imagery with AI tools (Gemini / Nano Banana Pro, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, or similar) required



- Strong prompt engineering skills: you can read a product brief and write a prompt that nails the scene, mood, and composition on the first or second pass



- Hands-on experience with AI image-to-video tools such as Kling, Veo 3/3.1, Runway, or Pika, with a portfolio of short clips you've produced



- Proficient in Adobe Photoshop, Canva, or Lightroom for retouching and finishing AI outputs (fixing artifacts, color correction, compositing) and for ad layout and text treatment



- A sharp eye for photorealism: you can spot the tells of AI-generated work (warped elements, melted textures, unnatural shadows, off proportions on furniture and textiles, etc.) and fix them before anyone else sees the file



- Obsessive attention to product accuracy: when comparing your AI output to the actual product photo, you catch the small stuff (a print motif that's slightly off, a flange width that's wrong by a quarter inch, a color that's a shade too warm) and fix it before delivery. The bar is exact match to the real product, not "looks close enough"



- Excellent English skills with high attention to detail and meticulous proofreading of any text or copy in your creatives



- A genuine sense of style and taste in interior design, home decor, and product photography; you understand why a room looks expensive vs. AI-generated stock



- Self-motivated, organized, and professional, with the ability to multi-task and prioritize across multiple brands and brief types; able to work independently with minimal supervision



- Comfortable working with our team via
, Slack, and Zoom. Highly preferred, but not required: experience with Airtable, Notion, Slack, and Google Drive



- Full-time position, 40 hrs/week



- Commitment to the company's mission and values, which can be found here: 



- Excel as a team player, contributing positively to our collective success.





ABOUT HIEND ACCENTS



HiEnd Accents designs and sells premium bedding and home textiles (quilts, sheets, decorative pillows, duvets, and more) that bring everyday luxury into people's homes. We've been family-owned and operated since 2005, now in our second generation of leadership, and are headquartered in Dallas, Texas.



We serve thousands of retailers, interior designers, department stores, and boutique hotels across the United States through four distinct brands spanning a range of styles and price points. Our growth has been recognized nationally: we've been named to the Inc. 5000 (a prestigious list of America's fastest-growing private companies) two years running, and have received multiple industry awards for product excellence.



We're a company of around 45 people, small enough that every person matters, established enough that you're joining something real and growing.



Discover more at 
.





WHY HIEND ACCENTS?



We're growing our team in the Philippines because we believe in finding the best people regardless of geography, and investing in them for the long term.



As a family-owned company, we don't treat our tea
mbers as interchangeable. We take the time to build real working relationships, we care about your professional development, and we want every person who works with us to leave with stronger skills than when they started. That's a genuine commitment from our leadership, not a line in a job posting.



What you can expect:



You'll work directly with our core team in Texas, not through layers of management or a BPO intermediary. Your contributions will be visible, your ideas heard, and your growth supported.



- Compensation & Benefits: Competitive pay benchmarked to PH market rates for skilled roles, not entry-level floors. Paid time off including vacation days and holidays, plus 13th-month pay.



- Real Career Growth: We're expanding quickly, which means new roles and responsibilities open up regularly. We actively develop our people through mentorship, skill-building, and increasing ownership of projects.



- A Team That Feels Like a Team: We're a close, collaborative group that celebrates wins together. You'll know your teammates by name, including leadership.





HiEnd is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.



For more information about us, please visit 
.

title: Paid Ads Media Buyer - Join DisruptorsMedia
description:
Read this first

This role is hands-on paid media for service business clients. You manage live ad accounts on Google and Meta, you own CPL and lead quality, and you defend your numbers in front of clients every week. We are not hiring a strategist who reviews dashboards and writes opinions. We are hiring an operator who runs the accounts.



If you have run service business lead gen accounts and can show us your CPLs by vertical, this is a great seat. If your only experience is e-commerce ROAS optimization, please skip this one.



Who we are

Disruptors Media is a Utah-based, AI-first marketing agency. We serve service businesses doing $300K or more in annual revenue: contractors, trades, medical, legal, and other professional service firms. Our founders run point as Fractional CMO and Chief AI Officer for our clients, and we build marketing systems and AI infrastructure inside the client's own software stack, not on top of someone else's vendor lock-in.



Learn more about how we work at 
.



Paid is one of our three highest-ROI engines. Our content engine drives inbound, our outbound engine fills the calendar, and paid is the lever we pull when a client needs volume now. You are the person who pulls it.



What this seat looks like day to day



Mornings are account check-ins across every live client: pacing against budget, CPL trends, lead quality flags from the sales teams, and any creative that hit fatigue overnight. Mid-day is build and optimize: new campaigns, ad copy iterations, audience refreshes, negative keyword sweeps, and bid adjustments. Afternoons mix client reporting, weekly strategy reviews, and feeding intelligence back to content and outbound on what messaging is converting.



You work inside Google Ads, Meta Ads Manager, GoHighLevel, Google Tag Manager, and our reporting stack. You also work inside our clients' CRMs to close the loop on lead quality and offline conversions.



What you will own



Direct management of five to fifteen client ad accounts across Google Ads (Search, Performance Max, YouTube), Meta 
 and Instagram), and occasionally LinkedIn. Hitting CPL and lead-quality targets by vertical, against benchmarks we will give you on day one.



Weekly client reports with real numbers and clear next actions. Lead quality monitoring through tagging, sales team feedback loops, and audience refinement based on which leads actually close. Conversion tracking setup and maintenance across GTM, Google Ads, Meta CAPI, and offline conversion imports from client CRMs. Landing page optimization recommendations to our WordPress team based on what the data is telling you.



What success looks like in your first 90 days



By day 30, you are managing three to five accounts under guidance, fluent in our reporting cadence, and posting weekly numbers without prompting. By day 60, you have a full book of clients, you are hitting CPL targets, and you are leading client calls solo. By day 90, you have a documented opinion on which campaign structures and audiences win in each vertical, and you are contributing to our internal playbook.



Required



Two or more years managing paid ad accounts with at least $250,000 in cumulative ad spend under your direct ownership. Documented numbers, not your team's numbers.



- Service business or local lead gen experience. Contractors, medical, legal, home services, professional services, or similar. You know the difference between a $40 CPL and a $400 CPL and which verticals carry which.



- Expert in Google Ads and Meta Ads. You build, you optimize, you do not just review.



- Defending CPL and CAC, not CTR and CPC. You speak in cost per qualified lead and customer acquisition cost.



- Conversion tracking competence. GTM, Google Ads conversions, Meta Pixel and CAPI, offline conversion imports.



- Strong client communication. You can run a weekly call, defend numbers, and recommend next actions clearly.



- Reliable infrastructure: high-speed internet, quiet workspace, US business hours overlap.



Nice to have



Performance Max with feed integration. Offline conversion imports wired into HubSpot, GoHighLevel, or similar CRMs. LinkedIn Ads. YouTube Ads beyond skippable in-stream. AI-assisted ad creative generation. Past agency experience with multiple concurrent clients. Google Ads or Meta certification. Familiarity with our stack: GoHighLevel, CallRail, Cloudflare, GTM.



Compensation and perks



Competitive base salary paid on a monthly or bi-monthly cycle. Performance bonus tied to client retention and account performance against CPL and lead quality targets. Uncapped upside on revenue from accounts you grow. Clear promotion path into senior media buyer or paid media lead for top performers.



Direct mentorship from the founders. You are in the room when strategy gets built, not after. Paid access to the full stack: ad platforms, reporting tools, AI creative tools, and the internal software we use to manage clients. We pay for your AI tooling because we expect you to use it.



We share specific base and bonus numbers on the first call, calibrated to your experience and your documented past performance.



This role is not for you if



Your wins are clicks and impressions instead of qualified leads. You are an e-commerce ROAS specialist with no service business reps. You do not want to be on weekly client calls. You want to strategize while someone else builds the campaigns. You expect to inherit perfect tracking and clean accounts on day one. You go quiet when CPLs spike and a client wants answers.



How to apply



Send all of the following to [hiring inbox]:



- Your resume or LinkedIn profile.



- Total ad spend you have managed directly in the last twelve months across Google, Meta, and any other platform.



- One case study, in six to eight sentences: the client or vertical, starting CPL, ending CPL, what you changed, and over what timeframe. No fluff



- A screenshot or anonymized export of one Google Ads or Meta account dashboard showing 30-day performance from work you owned. Redact client names if needed, keep the numbers visible.



- One sentence on the part of this job you expect to be hardest for you, and how you plan to manage it.



- Your expected base salary and any bonus or commission structure you want to discuss.



Complete applications get a response inside five business days. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.



Real budgets, real CPLs, real client calls. If that is the seat you want, send it over.

title: Accountant/CPA
description:
About Us

Inertia Rentals is a fast-growing independent rental car company focused on providing reliable vehicles to rideshare and gig-economy drivers. Our goal is to build a highly efficient operation with strong financial visibility and disciplined financial management.

We are looking for a detail-oriented Accountant or CPA who can help us maintain clean books, produce accurate financial reports, and provide insights that help the company grow profitably.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with operational businesses and wants to play a key role in building the financial systems behind a growing company.

Mission of the Role

The mission of this role is to build and maintain a reliable financial reporting system that gives leadership clear visibility into the company’s cash flow, profitability, and financial health.

Your work will help leadership make better decisions around fleet expansion, financing, and operational efficiency.

Key Outcomes (Scorecard)

Within the first 90 days, success in this role will look like:

• Accurate monthly financial statements prepared under GAAP

• Consistent cash flow statements and forecasting

• Clean and organized financial records

• Expense categorization and reconciliation completed monthly

• Financial reporting dashboard for leadership

• Clear documentation of accounting processes

Responsibilities

Financial Reporting

• Prepare monthly cash flow statements

• Generate Profit & Loss and Balance Sheet reports

• Maintain books according to GAAP accounting standards

Accounting Operations

• Reconcile bank accounts and credit cards

• Track vehicle-related expenses and depreciation

• Maintain accurate financial records in accounting software

Cash Flow Management

• Build cash flow tracking and forecasting models

• Monitor operational expenses and vehicle ROI

• Provide financial insights to leadership

Compliance

• Ensure financial records follow GAAP standards

• Assist with tax preparation documentation

• Maintain organized financial documentation

Process Improvement

• Create simple SOPs for accounting workflows

• Improve financial visibility and reporting efficiency

Requirements

• CPA preferred but not required

• 3+ years accounting experience

• Experience preparing cash flow statements

• Strong knowledge of GAAP accounting standards

• Experience with small business accounting systems (QuickBooks, Wave, Xero, etc.)

• Strong attention to detail and organization

• Ability to work independently in a remote environment

Nice to Have

• Experience with fleet management or rental car companies

• Experience with cash flow forecasting for asset-heavy businesses

• Startup or small business experience

title: L2 Technical Support - Spanish Speaking
description:
About DTiQ

DTiQ is a global leader in intelligent video-based surveillance and loss prevention solutions, supporting over 45,000 businesses worldwide—including major brands like Burger King, KFC, and McDonald’s. With over 20 years of innovation, DTiQ enhances more than 8 million customer experiences daily.

We are expanding our Philippines-based team and looking for highly skilled technical professionals who thrive in a fast-paced, customer-focused environment.

________________________________________

Role Overview

The Tier 2 Technical Support Specialist (L2 Support) serves as an escalation point for complex technical issues and plays a critical role in delivering high-quality customer experiences. This role is ideal for candidates with strong troubleshooting expertise across hardware, software, and networking, combined with experience in BPO, helpdesk, or managed services environments.



Application Link: 

________________________________________

Key Responsibilities

• Provide advanced technical support via phone,
, chat, and ticketing systems

• Troubleshoot and resolve complex hardware, software, and network-related issues

• Perform remote installation, configuration, and system diagnostics of DTiQ systems

• Act as an escalation point for Tier 1 agents and unresolved cases

• Collaborate with internal teams (Engineering, Product) for issue resolution

• Ensure compliance with SLAs, KPIs, and ticket queue performance metrics

• Accurately document troubleshooting steps, root cause analysis, and resolutions

• Contribute to knowledge base articles and continuous process improvement

________________________________________

Work Schedule

• Willing to support European working hours

________________________________________

Required Qualifications

• 2+ years technical support experience (L1/L2, Helpdesk, or BPO Tech Support)

• Fluent in Spanish

• Strong English communication skills (verbal and written)

• Solid knowledge of:

- Windows OS and basic system administration

- Networking fundamentals (TCP/IP, DNS, routers, connectivity troubleshooting)

• Experience using ticketing systems (e.g., Zendesk, ServiceNow, Jira, Freshdesk)

• Proven ability to troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues

• Experience working in a metrics-driven environment (SLA, AHT, CSAT)

• Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team setting

________________________________________

How to Apply?

As part of our standard recruitment procedure, we kindly ask that you complete your application through Rippling, our global Human Resources Information System (HRIS). This platform is used globally at DTiQ to ensure a consistent and secure way of managing candidate information.



Application Link: 

________________________________________

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in L2 or escalation support roles

Exposure to: Video surveillance / CCTV systems, POS systems or retail technologies, SaaS or cloud-based platforms

Technical certifications (e.g., CompTIA A+, Network+, ITIL)

Bachelor’s degree in IT, Computer Science, or related field (or equivalent experience)



________________________________________

What We Offer 

• Competitive salary package (based on experience)

• Night differential

• HMO + government-mandated benefits (SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG)

• Exposure to global clients and advanced technologies

• Collaborative and growth-focused work environment

________________________________________

Why Join DTiQ?

Join a globally recognized technology company where you’ll work on meaningful problems, develop advanced technical skills, and grow your career in a high-impact support role.



For more information on DTiQ visit our website (
) or our LinkedIn page (www.
/products/dtiq/).

________________________________________

Our Recruitment Process

Once your profile is complete in Rippling, we’ll begin to schedule interviews with qualified candidates. The interview will last up to an hour and may include 1-2 people (the direct hiring manager and potentially a department lead).

Hired candidates will be engaged through an Employment of Record (EOR) at the start of employment. This is a widely used approach by international companies to hire talend in countries where we do not yet have a legal entity, but allows us to comply with legal regulations. This means that Rippling, our EOR provider in the Philippines will officially employ you on behalf of DTiQ Technologies while you will work day to day with our team. Under this arrangement salary, government contributions and taxes are processed in compliance with Philippine regulations. Your formal contract will come from Ripping and DTiQ will manager your daily work, performance and career development. If you have further questions about this arrangement, please let me know and I’d be happy to address it with you.

title: E-commerce Operations Lead
description:
Ikigai Cases is hiring for an Ecommerce Operations Lead

Location: Remote

Compensation: $1800 per month + performance-based bonuses



About the Company

Ikigai Cases (
) is a family-owned e-commerce business selling premiu
tal pill cases. Founded in December 2021, the business has been operated by two brothers from Washington, DC since then. After four years, the company has grown to selling thousands of pill cases every month and double-digit tea
mbers, including a thriving remote team.



Our mission is simple: to make exceptional pill cases and provide exceptional customer service. We uphold this commitment by managing our fulfillment operations in-house and offering white-glove service to our customers.



We are looking to hire an E-commerce Operations Lead (Ecommerce Operations Manager) to join our growing remote team.



5 Core Responsibilities

1. Launch Execution: Plan and execute all sales, product launches, color launches, and promotional 
nts, ensuring offers, timing, assets, and systems go live correctly.



2. Cross-Functional Launch Coordination: Coordinate all teams involved in launches (Marketing,
, Creative, Website, Ops, CX, Fulfillment), managing timelines and dependencies to ensure aligned execution.



3. Website & Systems Setup for Launches: Set up and configure all website, Shopify, and related systems required for launches, including products, collections, discounts, carts, and launch-specific site changes.



4. Launch QA & System Hygiene: Own pre-launch QA and post-launch cleanup, ensuring accuracy across offers and flows and maintaining clean, current systems with no leftover artifacts.



5. Launch Process Improvement: Improve launch workflows, templates, and safeguards to support increasing launch volume and complexity with reduced risk.



Why You Should Take This Role

-You’ll be working at a brand that is obsessed with their customers and has built a loyal following. Just read a few of their reviews: 
/pages/reviews

-You’ll be working at a company that has scaled up extremely quickly - while focusing on the operational side of the business. Now that the operations are stabilized with key roles in place, we are focused on investing in growth.

-You’ll be working with people who have extreme levels of competitive fire in their bellies - people who will run through walls to make sure the company succeeds.

-You’ll be working hand-in-hand with the founders of the company - building the systems and growth strategy together.

-You’ll be working at a company with a robust product development roadmap that includes new colors / products releasing monthly



Required Experience & Skills Experience

-3+ years working directly on an e-commerce brand, with hands-on responsibility for executing sales, product launches, or promotional 
nts (not just website maintenance).

-Proven experience owning launches end-to-end, including setup, coordination, QA, and post-launch cleanup across teams and systems.

-Strong project management experience in e-commerce, coordinating multiple stakeholders to deliver time-sensitive launches.

-Working proficiency with Shopify admin for launch setup (products, collections, discounts, carts, and site changes; no coding required).

-Clear written communication and high attention to detail in revenue-critical work.

-Experience working with Notion or similar project management tools

-Must be based outside the United States.



Preferred Experiences (Nice To Have’s)

-Experience using page builders (Replo preferred) in addition to Shopify Theme Editor



Communication & English Proficiency

-Strong written and verbal English skills.

-Ability to communicate effectively with internal teams and external partners via
.

-Ability to receive and provide constructive feedback.



Compensation Details

-Compensation: $1800 per month + performance-based bonuses

-Work Hours: 50 hours per week, Monday to Friday Business Hours, EST

-Paid Holidays: 10 paid holidays per year

-PTO: 1 week earned for every 6 months worked at the company



To Apply

Fill out the application form here: 
/jobs/c993b7cc-298c-4817-b
bd5842

title: Full-Time Sales Representative (Remote) – AZ Move Masters (U.S. Moving Company)
description:
About Us

AZ Move Masters is one of Arizona’s fastest-growing luxury moving companies. We handle high-end residential and commercial moves with a white-glove standard of service. We’re expanding fast and looking for a dialed-in, experienced Sales Representative to join our remote team full-time.

???? The Role

You’ll be the voice of AZ Move Masters — taking inbound and follow-up calls, texting and
ing clients, preparing quotes, and locking in bookings daily. You’ll also handle agreements, scheduling confirmations, and post-sale follow-ups to ensure 5-star customer experiences.

This is a fast-paced, high-accountability role — we’re looking for someone who thrives in a results-driven environment.

???? Key Responsibilities

Take inbound and follow-up sales calls (via OpenPhone / GoHighLevel CRM)

Build quotes accurately using our pricing sheet

Send estimates, booking links, and service agreements

Ensure all customer communication is clear, professional, and timely

Availability: Must overlap with Arizona time (MST), especially 8AM–6PM

Saturdays as needed (rotational)

???? Compensation

Paid weekly via Wise/Payoneer

???? What We’re Looking For

Excellent English (spoken & written) — U.S. accent preferred

Strong sales experience (ideally in home services, moving, real estate, or similar)

Confident on the phone and fast with CRM tools

Reliable, proactive, and motivated to grow with our team

Your Vocaroo voice recording link (introduce yourself and do a short sample sales pitch — 30–60 seconds)

Your internet speed test link

Your current availability (days & hours in your time zone)

???? Applications without a Vocaroo will not be considered.

Why Join AZ Move Masters

We’re not your typical moving company. We’re building a modern, fast-scaling brand with an elite culture. If you’re hungry, driven, and love closing deals — this is where you level up.

title: Wedding Media Coordinator
description:
About the Company



We are a wedding photography and videography company focused on delivering a seamless, stress-free experience for our couples. We don’t just capture weddings; we manage the entire experience behind the scenes so our clients can enjoy their day without worry.  



The Role



We are looking for a Wedding Media Coordinator to be the main point of contact for our clients after they have paid their deposit. You will act as the bridge between our clients, photographers, videographers, and editors to make sure nothing gets missed from booking to final delivery.  



What You Will Be Responsible For



Client & Team Communication



- Take over communication once the client is booked and the deposit is paid.  

- Communicate primarily via
, text, and our client portal.  

- Phone Calls: While rare, you must be comfortable and available to jump on a phone call with a client or tea
mber if a situation requires it.

- Write the word Coordinator as the first word of your response so I know you read this whole thing.

- Keep clients informed, reassured, and guided throughout the entire process.  

- Coordinate with photographers, videographers, and editors to ensure they have accurate wedding details.  



Workflow Management



- Assign photographers and videographers to upcoming weddings.  

- Post new projects for tea
mbers to sign up for.  

- Manage and update the client portal, which serves as our main communication hub.  

- Double-check that no tasks, files, or timelines are missed.  



Post-Wedding Coordination



- Collect and organize media from the wedding team.  

- Send files to the editing team and monitor their progress.  

- Deliver final media to clients and maintain communication until the project is complete.  



Social Media Management



- Post company-provided wedding photos and videos.  

- Create captions and content aligned with current wedding trends.  

- Help maintain a professional and high-quality brand presence online.  



The "Weekend Hero" Bonus ($150+ Per I
)

Your core schedule is Monday to Friday. However, weddings happen on weekends.



- In 6 years and thousands of weddings, we have only had 2 major weekend emergencies. They are very rare.

- You are required to be reachable for these rare "Tier-1" emergencies (e.g., a photographer doesn't show up).

- The Reward: If you are called to resolve a weekend emergency, you will receive a $150+ Hero Bonus per i
 on top of your regular salary.



Schedule



- Monday to Friday

- 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (Assigned US Timezone)

- Weekend availability only for rare, urgent emergencies.



Who We Are Looking For



- Customer Service Experience: You should be comfortable talking to people and handling their concerns warmly and professionally.  

- Reliable & Organized: You are good at following through on tasks without being reminded.  

- Calm Under Pressure: You can handle a situation logically if things move fast.  

- Great Writer: You can write professional
s and creative social media captions.  

- Phone Ready: You are willing to get on a call when a text or
 isn't enough.



Why This Role Matters



This role is the backbone of our operations. When you do your job well, our team stays prepared and our clients feel taken care of every step of the way.  THIS IS A LONG TERM POSITION, NOT A GIG.



To Apply:



- Send a CV, Canva, loom video, written explanation or whatever you want telling me WHY you are the best fit for this role.

title: Virtual Assistant (Sales and Outreach Support)
description:
We are a fast-growing residential & commercial cleaning company looking for a proactive and reliable Virtual Assistant to join our team. This role will serve as a backup to our main sales rep and handle additional outreach/communication tasks.

Responsibilities:

Answer inbound calls using our sales scripts (when main sales rep is unavailable)

Respond to customer inquiries via text,
, and social media messages

Follow up with leads to ensure no missed opportunities

Perform cold outreach to potential customers (via
, text, or social platforms)

Maintain organized records of communications and update our CRM/system daily

Assist with administrative tasks as needed

Requirements:

Excellent English communication skills (both spoken and written)

Comfortable handling phone calls and following a proven sales script

Organized, detail-oriented, and proactive in following up with leads

Experience with customer service, sales, or appointment setting preferred

Reliable internet connection and quiet work environment

Wage/Salary: $3–$4 USD per hour (negotiable based on experience)

Hours: Flexible, but must be available during US business hours (Arizona time zone). Approx. 20–40 hours per week.

title: Admin Video Assistant
description:
We’re looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Virtual Executive Assistant who can support daily operations, create AI-generated videos, and handle basic video editing tasks.



This role is ideal for someone who has strong common sense, follows instructions well, and communicates clearly in English. You will be working directly with the founder and must be able to execute tasks from Loom video instructions with minimal hand-holding.



Responsibilities



1. AI Video Creation

Use AI tools (e.g., CapCut, Veo3) to create short-form video ads

Turn scripts or ideas into engaging visual content

Iterate quickly based on feedback



2. Video Editing (IMPORTANT)

Edit short-form videos (TikTok/Instagram/Reels style)

Add captions, cuts, subtitles.

Follow references and replicate styles



3. Admin & Execution

Organize files, links, and assets

Follow Loom video instructions precisely

Handle simple operational tasks (naming files, uploading content, etc.)

Communicate progress clearly and proactively



Requirements:

Strong English comprehension (must understand Loom instructions clearly)

Proven video editing ability (CapCut, Premiere Pro, or similar)

Familiarity with AI tools (or ability to learn fast)

High attention to detail

Strong common sense and problem-solving ability

Reliable and consistent



Work Schedule:

Monday to Friday

11:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST



Compensation:

$3/hour

Performance-based bonuses available for strong execution and reliability





Application Instructions (READ CAREFULLY)

To apply, you must complete the test below. If you do not follow instructions exactly, your application will be ignored:

1. In your reply/subject line, include the word: “banana”

2. Write 2–3 sentences explaining why you are a good fit for this role.

title: Part-Time Success Advisor / Community Engagement Specialist for Brand U Academy — Remote Role
description:
Yes. Here is the **Brand U Success Advisor job posting** you can copy and paste into OnlineJobs.ph.



---



# Success Advisor / Community Engagement Specialist



## Brand U Academy | Part-Time Remote Position



Brand U Academy was founded by Best-Selling Author and Award-Winning Digital and Brand Management Consultant **Glenda Boone**.



Brand U Academy is an online entrepreneurial school that helps female entrepreneurs, creatives, authors, coaches, and women in ministry gain clarity, structure, and strategy so they can develop, manage, and monetize their brands.



We are looking for a warm, organized, and mission-driven **Success Advisor** to join our growing team.



At Brand U Academy, our customer service tea
mbers are called **Success Advisors** because they do more than answer questions. They help guide women, encourage students, support the community, assist the Integrator/COO, and make sure every student feels seen, supported, and celebrated.



This is a **part-time remote position to start**, with the opportunity to grow into full-time, student success, community management, operations, or leadership opportunities.



---



## Role Overview



The Success Advisor will support Brand U Academy students, leads, and members throughout their journey.



This person will work closely with the **Sales Representative** to help conduct **Brand Clarity Calls**, follow up with leads, answer questions, and guide potential students to their best next step.



They will also assist the **Integrator/COO** with student success, onboarding, Open Office Hours, community engagement, CRM updates, follow-up tasks, and day-to-day operational support.



This person will help make sure students are not just welcomed into Brand U Academy, but also guided, encouraged, celebrated, and supported after they join.



In addition, this person will help manage the online Brand U community by posting encouraging messages, birthday flyers, student wins, reminders, and engagement prompts that keep the community active and connected.



This role is part student support, part sales support, part community engagement, and part operations assistance.



---



## Primary Responsibilities



The Success Advisor will:



* Conduct or assist with **Brand Clarity Calls** with leads and prospects.

* Work with the Sales Representative to support the sales and enrollment process.

* Help prospects identify what they are building, where they feel stuck, and what support they need.

* Guide prospects toward the appropriate Brand U Academy next step.

* Respond to student and lead questions by text,
, phone, chat, or CRM conversations.

* Help book, confirm, and follow up after Brand Clarity Calls.

* Nurture leads who are interested but not ready to purchase.

* Follow up with people who registered for live sessions, attended events, missed calls, or requested help.

* Support students with onboarding, portal access, community access, and event preparation.

* Co-host onboarding sessions with the Integrator/COO.

* Co-host Open Office Hours with the Integrator/COO.

* Take notes during onboarding sessions, Open Office Hours, and student support conversations.

* Help track student needs, questions, wins, and follow-up items.

* Update contact records inside Go High Level.

* Add notes, tags, tasks, and follow-up reminders in the CRM.

* Assist the Integrator/COO with administrative, operational, and student success tasks.

* Monitor student needs and escalate important issues to leadership.

* Help manage the online community.

* Post positive messages, encouragement, reminders, and engagement prompts.

* Create or post birthday flyers for students and members.

* Celebrate student wins inside the community.

* Encourage members to participate, comment, share wins, and stay connected.

* Help maintain a warm, uplifting, and faith-friendly community environment.

* Support the Brand U mission by helping women feel encouraged, clear, and connected.



---



## Operations Support Responsibilities



The Success Advisor will also assist the Integrator/COO with important student success and operations tasks.



This may include:



* Co-hosting onboarding sessions with the Integrator/COO.

* Helping welcome new students and explain what to expect inside Brand U Academy.

* Supporting students during onboarding by answering basic questions.

* Making sure new members understand where to find resources, training, community links, and next steps.

* Co-hosting Open Office Hours with the Integrator/COO.

* Helping monitor questions during Open Office Hours.

* Taking notes during student support calls, onboarding sessions, and Open Office Hours.

* Tracking student questions, concerns, and follow-up needs.

* Helping the Integrator/COO identify students who may need extra support.

* Making sure action items from onboarding sessions and Open Office Hours are documented.

* Updating Go High Level with student notes, tags, tasks, and follow-up reminders.

* Helping prepare reminders for onboarding sessions, Open Office Hours, bootcamps, and live trainings.

* Supporting the overall student experience so members feel guided, not lost.



---



## Community Engagement Responsibilities



The Success Advisor will help keep the Brand U community active, positive, and encouraging.



This may include:



* Posting morning motivation or weekly encouragement.

* Sharing birthday graphics or celebration posts.

* Highlighting student wins.

* Welcoming new members.

* Reminding students about upcoming calls, bootcamps, trainings, or deadlines.

* Encouraging members to introduce themselves.

* Asking simple engagement questions.

* Commenting on student posts to make members feel seen.

* Notifying the team when a student needs extra support.

* Helping maintain the tone and culture of the Brand U Sisterhood.



The goal is to make sure students do not just join Brand U Academy — they feel like they belong.



---



## Brand Clarity Call Purpose



Brand Clarity Calls are quick support calls designed to help leads feel more confident before attending a live session, joining Bootcamp, or moving forward with Brand U Academy.



The Success Advisor may use language such as:



**“Want help before the live session? Book a quick Brand Clarity Call with a Success Advisor.”**



During the call, the Success Advisor helps the lead understand:



* What they are trying to build.

* Why they may feel stuck.

* What kind of support they need.

* Whether Brand U Academy is a good fit.

* What their next step should be.



The goal is to create clarity, build trust, and help the right women move forward.



---



## Required Skills



The ideal candidate should have:



* Strong written and verbal English communication skills.

* A warm, friendly, and professional communication style.

* Good listening skills.

* Strong attention to detail.

* Confidence having guided conversations by phone or Zoom.

* Ability to ask good questions and take clear notes.

* Ability to follow scripts while still sounding natural.

* Comfort responding by text,
, phone, and CRM inbox.

* Comfort posting inside an online community.

* Ability to create or post simple graphics such as birthday flyers or celebration posts.

* Good organization and follow-up habits.

* Patience with students who may feel confused, overwhelmed, or unsure.

* Basic computer and internet skills.

* Ability to update notes, tags, and records inside a CRM.

* A positive, encouraging, and faith-friendly communication style.

* Willingness to learn Brand U Academy’s offers, systems, student journey, and community culture.



---



## Preferred Experience



Experience in any of the following is a plus:



* Student success

* Customer service

* Community management

* Online course support

* Membership support

* Sales support

* Appointment setting

* Discovery calls

* Coaching program support

* Go High Level / GHL

* CRM management

* Canva or basic graphic creation

*
 Groups,
, or online communities

* Working with coaches, consultants, ministries, or small businesses

* Supporting a COO, Integrator, or operations manager



Go High Level experience is preferred, but we are willing to train the right person.



---



## Technology You May Use



The Success Advisor may work with:



* Go High Level / Brand U Engine

* CRM pipelines

* Calendar booking links

* Phone and SMS tools

*
 follow-up systems

* AI Sales Team tools

* Student portals

*
 or online communities

*
 Groups

* Canva

* Google Docs or Google Sheets

* Brand Clarity Call scripts

* Internal SOPs and checklists



You do not need to know every tool before starting, but you must be willing to learn and work inside a tech-supported student success environment.



---



## Work Schedule



This is a **part-time remote position** to start.



The schedule may include daytime or evening availability depending on Brand Clarity Call bookings, live session schedules, student needs, onboarding sessions, Open Office Hours, and community activity.



The ideal person is dependable, responsive, organized, and consistent during agreed-upon hours.



There is room for this role to grow into a full-time position as Brand U Academy continues to expand.



---



## Compensation



Compensation will be discussed during the interview process based on experience, skill level, and availability.



This role may begin as an hourly part-time position, with potential for performance bonuses or growth-based incentives as responsibilities increase.



---



## Growth Opportunity



The right person may have the opportunity to grow into:



* Full-time Success Advisor

* Lead Success Advisor

* Community Engagement Manager

* Student Success Coordinator

* Enrollment Support Specialist

* Operations Assistant

* Integrator/COO Support Specialist

* Client Success Manager

* Brand U Community Manager



We are looking for someone who can grow with the Brand U Academy mission and help build a world-class support experience for our students and prospects.



---



## Company Culture



Brand U Academy is a supportive, purpose-driven, and fast-growing company.



We believe in clarity, faith, structure, excellence, community, systems, and transformation.



Our team culture is warm, professional, innovative, and focused on helping women succeed without losing who they are.



We value people who are:



* Kind

* Coachable

* Organized

* Honest

* Mission-minded

* Detail-oriented

* Solution-focused

* Comfortable with technology

* Strong communicators

* Comfortable with people and systems



---



## Who This Role Is Best For



This role is a good fit for someone who:



* Loves helping people feel supported.

* Enjoys encouraging women.

* Is comfortable talking on the phone or Zoom.

* Can follow up without being reminded.

* Enjoys posting positive community content.

* Can celebrate student wins and birthdays.

* Is organized with notes, tags, and CRM updates.

* Can support both people and systems.

* Likes working behind the scenes to help things run smoothly.

* Wants to be part of a mission-driven company.



---



## Who This Role Is Not For



This role may not be a good fit if you:



* Do not enjoy helping people.

* Do not like phone calls or follow-up.

* Struggle to stay organized.

* Are uncomfortable posting in online communities.

* Do not like using a CRM.

* Need constant reminders to complete tasks.

* Are not comfortable with faith-friendly, women-centered messaging.

* Prefer only administrative work with no people interaction.



---



## Sample Community Post



**Good morning, Brand U Sisters!**



Today is a beautiful day to take one clear step toward what God placed in your hands.



Drop one thing below that you are working on this week — your book, your business, your ministry, your offer, your website, or your content.



We want to celebrate your progress, not just your finished product.



---



## Sample Brand Clarity Call Message



**Hi {{contact.first_name}}, this is [Name], one of the Success Advisors with Brand U Academy. I saw that you registered for the live session, and I wanted to personally invite you to book a quick Brand Clarity Call before class.**



**This quick call will help you get clear on what you’re building, where you may feel stuck, and what your best next step may be before the live session.**



**Would you like me to send you the booking link?**



---



## How to Apply



Interested candidates should complete the application using the link below:









In your application, please include:



* Your student support, customer service, or community management experience.

* Your experience conducting calls, discovery calls, or support calls.

* Your experience with Go High Level or other CRM systems, if any.

* Your experience posting in online communities.

* Your experience with Canva or simple graphics, if any.

* Why you believe you would be a great fit for Brand U Academy.

* Your availability to work 6:00 pm - 9 Pm EST



**Part-Time Success Advisor / Community Engagement Specialist for Brand U Academy — Remote Role**

title: Product Image Retoucher & 3D/AI Visualisation Specialist (Furniture)
description:
We are a  UK-based furniture brand seeking a highly skilled image editing and AI visualisation specialist to support our ongoing creative work.  

This is a specialist role requiring both technical precision and a strong design eye.

Scope of Work:

You will be working with high-quality sofa and furniture imagery to:

Cut out raw product images to a flawless, professional standard

Enhance and retouch images to achieve a rich, high-end aesthetic

Accurately change colours and textures (fabric & leather)

Place products into realistic, well-styled interior settings

Create convincing lifestyle scenes that reflect a luxury brand

Produce 360° animations / product spins

Use AI tools to elevate quality, realism, and efficiency



Essential Requirements

Proven experience in high-end product image editing (furniture preferred)

Advanced skills in tools such as:

Adobe Photoshop

Adobe Lightroom

Blender or equivalent 3D tools

Experience with AI image tools (e.g. Midjourney, DALL·E, or similar workflows)

Strong understanding of lighting, perspective, and interior styling

Ability to produce photo-realistic room scenes (not obviously “edited”)

Experience creating 360° product views or animations



Portfolio Proof (Non-Negotiable)

“Please provide 3–5 examples of your BEST work that include:

Background cut-outs (before & after)

Furniture placed into room scenes

Colour or material changes

Any 360° or animation work (if available)

Applications without relevant examples will not be considered.”



The job will involve ongoing freelance work

title: Position  Head of Customer Support (Part-Time)
description:
About us

We are a fast-growing premium health brand based in the German-speaking market (DACH region), currently generating around €50,000/month in revenue. Our vision is to become a long-term lifestyle brand — one that continuously grows its customer base, develops its products, and builds a true brand identity in the German market. We achieve this through strong customer relationships, ongoing product development, and smart marketing.



The role

We are looking for a Head of Customer Support who will take full ownership of our customer communication. Right now, we receive around 15 messages per day across
 and WhatsApp — plus occasional phone calls. You will handle all of this independently and make sure every customer has a great experience. Your work directly protects our brand reputation and helps prevent chargebacks.



Why this role matters to the team

By taking full ownership of customer support, you free up the rest of our team to focus entirely on creating high-quality ad copy and marketing. More and better ads = more revenue. More revenue = better pay for everyone on the team — including you. Your role is the foundation that makes our growth possible.



This is a role that grows with us

Right now, we are offering approximately 1–2 hours of work per day. We are currently working on fixing key backend issues that are holding back our full growth potential. Once resolved, our revenue will increase — and with it, the size of our team, the quality of our infrastructure, and the compensation for everyone involved. We are looking for someone who wants to grow with us from the ground up, not just fill a short-term role.



What you will do

Independently handle all customer
s via Outlook

Manage WhatsApp Business chat support on a daily basis

Take phone calls when needed

Assess situations and d
 the right response — whether to offer a partial refund, a full refund, or de-escalate with good communication

Flag urgent or high-risk cases to the founder immediately

Proactively share feedback and ideas to improve our customer support processes

Must-have skills

Excellent German language skills — both written and spoken (our customers are German-speaking)

Strong communication skills — clear, calm, and professional with customers at all times

Good judgment — knowing when to escalate a case and when to handle it directly

Self-starter mindset — you work independently without needing to be managed

Minimum 6 months of hands-on customer support experience

Nice to have

Experience working with e-commerce or health/wellness brands

Familiarity with Shopify

Experience in chargeback prevention

Tools we use

Outlook 
)

WhatsApp Business

Shopify

Notion (team communication & documentation)

Work setup

Part-time, approx. 1–2 hours per day to start — with clear potential to grow

Available between 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM German time (CET/CEST)

Remote / hybrid setup

Rate: ~$5/hour (fixed hourly rate)

How we measure success

Return rate kept low

Positive customer feedback

Chargeback rate minimized

Response time within business hours

Compensation

Fixed hourly rate of ~$5/hr. We also offer performance bonuses for great work. If you bring new ideas, improve our processes, and help us grow — you will be rewarded. The better the team performs as a whole, the better everyone gets paid. We grow together. 





How to apply

Send us a DM directly. Please include:



A short Loom video introducing yourself and your experience

Examples of brands or companies you have worked with

How you handle specific customer support situations (refunds, complaints, escalations)

How you manage translation and language challenges

A brief overview of how you approach your work in general

title: GoHighLevel Agency Specialist / White Label Setup Expert
description:
We are launching a white-label digital marketing agency called Deep Eddy Digital based in Austin, TX. We help local service businesses — plumbers, electricians, hairdressers, landscapers, and more — get found online, never miss a call, and grow through websites, AI receptionists, automated booking, and digital marketing. All delivered white-glove under our brand.

We need an experienced GoHighLevel specialist to build out our agency account from scratch and get us client-ready. This is a project-based engagement (20-25 hours) with the opportunity to stay on as our ongoing admin VA at 5-10 hours per week.

WHAT YOU'LL BUILD:



Full white-label agency account setup including branding and custom domain

SaaS Mode configuration with 3 pricing tiers

Master Snapshot template including AI voice agents, website template, workflows, automations, CRM pipeline, and intake forms

Our agency website built inside GHL

Prospecting CRM and outreach
 sequences

TO APPLY: Please send examples or links to previous GHL agency builds you've completed, your hourly rate, and estimated hours to complete the full project. A Loom walkthrough of previous work is a bonus but not required.

This is a long-term opportunity. Once the initial build is complete we'll keep you on retainer for ongoing client onboarding, website updates, and sub-account management.
