title: Patient Care Coordinator
description:
About the Company

Elite Sedation is a mobile dental anesthesia provider delivering safe, hospital-grade sedation services directly in dental offices. The company partners with dental practices to support in-office procedures by providing professional anesthesia care, ensuring patients experience safe, comfortable, and stress-free treatment. With a focus on patient safety and clinical excellence, Elite Sedation helps dental teams improve efficiency while maintaining high standards of care.



Job Overview

Elite Sedation is seeking a dedicated and detail-oriented Patient Coordinator to provide excellent support to patients, providers, and offices. This role is vital to ensuring a smooth patient experience—from scheduling, pre-op education to post-op follow-up and payment collection.

It is essential that the applicant demonstrates compassion and empathy toward patients, ensuring they feel supported, informed, and at ease throughout their care journey, while also maintaining strong professionalism in communication with partnered offices to support effective collaboration and continuity of care. This position also requires strong organizational skills, reliability, and the ability to manage a high-volume workflow with accuracy and attention to detail.

The Patient Coordinator will manage communication across multiple offices, handle provider schedules, and support families with professionalism and empathy. This position is based in California; however, we hire remote talent internationally.



Key Responsibilities

1. Patient Support & Communication

- Send welcome messages, reminders, and follow-ups to patients and families.

- Conduct pre-op calls/texts to review preparation, medical conditions, medications, and patient readiness.

- Provide attentive customer service to worried or high-need parents.

- Complete post-op calls and assist with follow-up questions or concerns.

- Manage incoming calls, voi
s, and texts in a professional manner.



2. Provider & Office Coordination

- Communicate with assigned providers daily via Slack, providing updates and case notes.

- Confirm office schedules, start times, and patient lists.

- Handle last-minute schedule changes and provide timely updates to both offices and families.

- Coordinate with offices regarding records, paperwork, and urgent scheduling needs.



3. Administrative & Records Management

- Collect and process patient payments through secure platforms.

- Request, track, and follow up on medical records and consents.

- Maintain accurate notes, patient charts, and Google Calendar updates.

- Ensure schedules and documentation are correct and up to date at all times.



4. Team Collaboration & Coverage

- Provide coverage for other Patient Coordinators when needed.

- Share updates and pending tasks with the team during shift changes.

- Work collaboratively in a high-volume environment, covering 20+ offices weekly with multiple patients per schedule.



Candidate Requirements (Required)

- Previous experience in dental or healthcare administration is preferred.

- Excellent communication (English) and interpersonal abilities.

- Strong organizational skills and meticulous attention to detail.

- Proven ability to multitask effectively and thrive in a fast-paced setting.

- A firm commitment to delivering outstanding patient care.



Technical/Work Environment Requirements (Required)

- Must have access to two computer screens.

- Must have a backup internet connection in case of power outages.



Key Traits

- Professional, empathetic, and patient-focused.

- Highly organized with sharp attention to detail.

- Adaptable and able to thrive under pressure.

- Reliable, proactive, and accountable in a remote work environment.

- Strong communicator and collaborative team player.



Work Schedule:

Eight (8) hours between 5:30AM-6:00PM PST (Monday-Friday)



If you are passionate about making a difference in the lives of patients and are committed to excellence, we encourage you to apply for the Patient Care Coordinator position at Elite Sedation. Join us in our mission to set the gold standard for anesthesia services through quality patient care.

This role demands speed, precision, and a strong command of modern data tools as we process millions of contacts each month. If you’re fast with tools like Clay and Apollo, obsessed with data quality, and know what makes a great B2B prospect list, you’ll thrive here.

Qualifications

title: AIRBNB SHORT LET PROPERTY MANAGER
description:
Commitment: Full-time (8 hours per day, 7 days per week)

Start Date: ASAP



About the Role:

We are a growing short-let property business based in the UK, managing multiple Airbnb listings. We are looking for a reliable and experienced Virtual Assistant (VA) to fully support and manage the day-to-day operations of our Airbnb properties.



This is a full-time role that requires a daily commitment (7 days a week), strong communication skills, and a solid understanding of the short-let rental business. You will be a key part of our team, ensuring smooth operations and excellent guest experiences.



Please note: This must be your only job. We require full attention and availability to support our operations.



Key Responsibilities:



Daily Tasks:



* Respond promptly to guest messages and booking inquiries on Airbnb and other platforms

* Confirm bookings and monitor availability calendars

* Review and update front-end listings daily to ensure accurate pricing, visibility, and bookability

* Coordinate with cleaning and maintenance teams to ensure properties are guest-ready

* Monitor guest check-ins and check-outs, ensuring smooth transitions and issue resolution

* Track and report daily task completion, guest interactions, and progress on ongoing issues

* Ensure keys and access details are properly managed and communicated



Weekly Tasks:



* Review upcoming reservations and coordinate logistics

* Follow up with vendors and cleaners for feedback and issue resolution

* Ensure supplies and consumables are stocked or restocked where needed

* Update guest manuals, listing descriptions, and SOPs if necessary

* Prepare and send weekly reports summarizing operations, occupancy, and performance



Monthly Tasks:



* Track KPIs such as occupancy rates, reviews, and guest satisfaction

* Assist with monthly expenses and supplier/vendor tracking

* Identify recurring problems and propose solutions to improve efficiency

* Contribute to operational improvements and SOP updates



Additional Responsibilities:



* Assist in sourcing new short-let units and growing the business

* Provide basic support with social media marketing and online visibility

* Support the business owner with general administrative tasks as needed



Requirements:



* At least 1 year of experience working as a VA for Airbnb or a short-let property management business

* Excellent written and spoken English

* Strong organisational and time-management skills

* Proficient with tools like Google Drive, Google Sheets, and online communication platforms

* Familiar with Airbnb (calendar, messaging, resolution centre, etc.)

* Self-motivated, dependable, and able to handle tasks independently

* Flexibility to handle urgent matters, especially during peak guest hours

* Must not have other job commitments; this is a dedicated full-time role



Working Hours:



* 8 hours per day, 7 days a week

* Flexible schedule, but availability must align with UK guest needs (mornings, afternoons, or evenings as required)

* Occasional urgent requests may need handling outside regular hours



What We Offer:



* Long-term role with room for growth

* Clear documentation and training provided

* Supportive and collaborative work environment

* Potential for bonuses and pay increases based on performance



To Apply:

Please send your CV and a short cover letter explaining your experience with Airbnb and why you're a good fit for this role. Only applicants with direct experience in short-let or Airbnb property management will be considered.

The best candidate can answer phones and texts efficiently,  communicate effectively, staff shifts with our top-notch caregivers strategically, conduct initial intake calls with potential clients, and who can assist our HR Manager with hiring tasks.  We are looking for a well-rounded individual with in-home care experience serving the elderly. Merit-based increase in wage upon 90 day, 6-month, and annual reviews.

title: Customer Support Specialist (Troubleshooting & Technical Support)
description:
Company: Script Runner

Location: Remote (Canada)

Type: Full-time



About Script Runner

Script Runner is modernizing the infrastructure behind pharmacy delivery. We power same-day prescription delivery for pharmacies across Canada, combining software, automation, and last-mile logistics to create a seamless patient experience.

We operate a large, distributed driver network and partner with hundreds of pharmacies. Our goal is simple: make medication delivery as reliable and fast as ordering anything online.



The Role

We’re looking for a Pharmacy Support Specialist to be the front line for our pharmacy clients.

You will handle real-time issues that pharmacies face with our software and driver fleet. You will ensure deliveries are completed smoothly for the pharmacies, and they can smoothly use our software to create and manage orders. This is a fast-paced, operations-heavy role where things can change quickly — you’ll need to think on your feet and take ownership.

This is not a passive support role. You are actively helping run the operation.



What You’ll Do



Real-Time Pharmacy Support for our logistics software.

Respond to incoming pharmacy issues via phone.

Troubleshoot delivery problems (Where is my driver? My pharmacy portal is not working…I can’t login etc)

Guide Pharmacies through edge cases and exceptions in real time.

This has nothing to do with medication etc, we handle medication delivery and logistics from the pharmacy to the customer home. The pharmacies use our logistic software - and this is what you will be helping them with.





Operational Coordination

Work closely with dispatch, operations, and pharmacies to resolve issues quickly

Escalate issues to the right people on the team to help you solve problems.

Ensure pharmacies are satisfied with our offering.





Issue Resolution

Investigate issues with the product, teach pharmacies how different features work

Communicate clearly with pharmacies on next steps

Document issues and resolutions in internal systems





Technical Experience

Support pharmacies with easy technical questions 

Provide basic guidance and troubleshooting

Maintain a professional and calm tone, even in high-pressure situations





Process Improvement

Identify recurring issues and suggest improvements

Help build better support workflows, templates, and automation

Work with internal tools (and eventually AI systems) to improve efficiency



What We’re Looking For

Core Traits

Strong ownership mindset — you don’t pass problems, you solve them

Technical in nature –(Using tools like dispatching, routing, google maps, whatsapp etc)

Fast and decisive — able to act quickly with incomplete information

Calm under pressure — things will go wrong, often in real time

Highly organized — can juggle multiple issues at once

Clear communicator — concise, direct, and professional





Experience

1–3 years in customer support, logistics, or operations (preferred)

Some technical support experience is helpful.

Experience in fast-paced environments (delivery, rideshare, retail ops, etc.) is a big plus





Familiarity with tools like ticketing systems, chat platforms, or dispatch software is helpful





Nice to Have

Experience supporting gig workers or driver fleets (Uber, Instacart, etc.)

Experience supporting software for clients.

Comfort working with data, dashboards, or operational tools

Interest in automation and AI-driven workflows







What Success Looks Like

Pharmacies feel supported and can resolve issues quickly

Deliveries are completed smoothly with minimal disruption

Issues are handled proactively, not reactively

You become someone the team relies on to “figure things out”



Compensation

Competitive salary based on experience

Working hours are 9am to 8pm EST, with 20-30 calls per day. 

Weekends if possible, but not required, 10am - 3pm EST.

Opportunity to grow







Why Join Script Runner

Be part of a fast-growing health tech company

Work on real-world logistics problems that matter

High ownership, low bureaucracy environment

Opportunity to shape how operations and support scale

title: Interior Design Construction Documentation Specialist
description:
Job Title: Architect – Interior Design Documentation Specialist (Fully Remote)

Schedule: 9:00 AM – 5:30 PM PST, Monday–Friday (12:00 AM – 8:30 AM PH Time)

Company: Alignment Architects Inc., Cupertino, CA

Location: Fully Remote



Company Introduction

Join our fast-growing architectural and design firm based in San Jose, backed by AlphaX RE Capital. We specialize in single-family homes, ADUs, SB9 projects, multi-family dwellings, and light industrial developments. With a full range of real estate services—from land acquisition to project execution—Alignment Architects Inc. offers a dynamic environment for collaboration, technical growth, and design excellence.



Position Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented and technically skilled Architect – Interior Design Documentation Specialist to support our residential projects in California. This role focuses on the preparation of high-quality interior design construction documents and post-permit revisions.



You will play a critical role in translating design intent into precise, buildable drawings, ensuring coordination across disciplines and compliance with applicable codes and standards. This position is ideal for someone with strong documentation experience who thrives in a fast-paced, production-driven environment.



Key Responsibilities

- Construction Documentation:

Develop and refine detailed interior design construction documents, including floor plans, reflected ceiling plans (RCPs), elevations, sections, schedules, and interior details.

- Technical Coordination:

Coordinate architectural interiors with structural, MEP (mechanical, electrical, plumbing), and civil drawings to ensure fully integrated and conflict-free documentation.

- Code Compliance:

Ensure all drawings comply with California building codes, local zoning regulations, and industry standards.

- Post-Permit Documentation:

Prepare supplemental drawings, revisions, and addendums to address plan check comments, RFIs, and construction changes.

- Design Translation:

Accurately translate design concepts, sketches, and markups from designers into clear and precise technical drawings.

- Team Collaboration:

Work closely with architects, interior designers, project managers, and consultants to maintain documentation accuracy and efficiency.

- Quality Control:

Review drawings for completeness, consistency, and constructability prior to submission.

- Software Utilization:

Produce high-quality drawings using industry-standard tools such as Revit, AutoCAD, and Bluebeam.



Qualifications

- Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Architecture

- Experience: Minimum of 3 years in architectural/interior design documentation (residential experience preferred; US projects a plus)

- Software Proficiency:

- Strong proficiency in Revit & Autocad (required)

- Knowledge of SketchUp and Bluebeam is an advantage

- Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite

- Technical Knowledge:

- Strong understanding of construction detailing and documentation standards

- Familiarity with MEP and structural coordination

- Knowledge of California codes is a plus



- Skills:

- Excellent written and verbal English communication

- Strong attention to detail and organization

- Ability to manage multiple deadlines in a fast-paced environment

- Proactive, collaborative, and accountable work ethic



If interested, send a message with your updated CV and portfolio. Make sure your CV and portfolio are visible to us (or accessible). Thanks!

title: Experienced Full-Time Accountant  (PHP 60,000.00/Month)
description:
Experienced Full-Time Bookkeeper / Accountant



Remote | Fire & Security Contracting / Engineering Company



We are a growing fire and security contracting and engineering company looking for an experienced Bookkeeper / Staff Accountant to help keep our finances accurate, our systems organized, and our business operations running smoothly.



This is not just a bookkeeping role. It is an opportunity for the right person to grow into a long-term finance leadership position within the company.



Key Responsibilities



Manage QuickBooks Online, including invoicing, payment tracking, account reconciliation, and financial recordkeeping.

Create, send, and monitor customer invoices.

Track unpaid invoices, estimates, job forms, and billing documents.

Upload invoices and track payments in ServiceChannel.

Review job completion forms to ensure accurate and complete billing.

Keep the books clean, organized, and ready for CPA review and tax filing.

Organize and audit Google Drive files using proper naming standards.

Ensure documents, paperwork, and financial files are routed to the correct stakeholders.

Maintain a high level of order, accuracy, and organization at all times.

Handle insurance documents, renewals, tracking, and related administrative records.

Create and maintain SOPs for finance and administrative workflows.

Track, maintain, and report key financial and operational KPIs.





Requirements



Must be available to work full-time, 40 hours per week.

Must be available and reachable during 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM Eastern Time / Eastern Daylight Time.

Expert-level experience with QuickBooks Online.

Multi-entity bookkeeping experience is preferred.

Strong knowledge of Google Sheets / Excel, including formulas, pivot tables, and reporting.

Proficient with Google Workspace, including Docs, Drive, Gmail, and Calendar.

Comfortable using Notion for documentation and task tracking.

Familiarity with Zoho CRM is highly preferred.

Minimum 5+ years of bookkeeping, accounting, or staff accountant experience.

Strong written and spoken English communication skills.

Experience creating SOPs and maintaining process documentation.

Able to track, maintain, and report KPIs.

Highly organized, detail-oriented, reliable, and proactive.

Able to work independently and solve problems without constant supervision.

Experience with ServiceChannel is a plus.





Position Details



Remote full-time position

40 hours per week

Schedule: 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM Eastern Time

Must be reachable during business hours

Long-term role for the right candidate





Growth & Career Path



We are building for the long term, and the right candidate will have the opportunity to grow with us.





This role may lead to:



Increased responsibilities in finance and operations

Promotion track toward Head of Finance Department

Long-term leadership opportunity within a growing, tech-enabled contracting and engineering company



This role is designed for someone who does not just want “a job,” but wants a real career path with growth potential.





Application Instructions



Please read carefully. To apply, you must complete all steps below.



Answer this logic/comprehension question in 2–3 sentences:



“If you are given 5 invoices to process, but 1 of them is missing supporting documents, what would you do?”



Use this exact subject line in your application:



CPA/Bookkeeper to – [Your Name]



Submit a 2-minute video explaining your experience and expertise in bookkeeping, staff accounting, or financial controlling.

You may upload the video through Google Drive or Loom and share the link in your application.



At the very end of your application, include this exact phrase:



“I want to synergize with you”





Important



Applications that do not follow the instructions above will not be reviewed. We are looking for someone who is detail-oriented, organized, and able to follow instructions carefully from the very beginning.

title: Senior GoHighLevel (GHL) Automation Expert
description:
Job Type: Part-time 

Schedule: Fully flexible — work when it suits you. We communicate async via Slack. No fixed hours.

Salary: $1,000 – $1,500/month USD 

Start Date: Immediate



About Us

We're LMD Growth Agency, a digital marketing agency based in South Florida. We manage paid search, paid social, SEO, web development, and marketing automation for a portfolio of established US clients across home services, automotive, healthcare, and retail. Our clients aren't startups — they're real businesses doing real revenue, and they expect real results.

We're not looking for a button-pusher or a generalist VA who "knows GHL." We're looking for a true expert — someone who lives in the platform, thinks in workflows and triggers, and knows the difference between an automation that looks good on paper and one that actually drives bookings.

If you've been the person at your agency who fixes everyone else's broken GHL setups, this role is for you.



What You'll Actually Do



Audit existing GHL sub-accounts across multiple clients and identify what's broken, underperforming, or missing entirely

Build and optimize end-to-end automations: lead intake, nurture sequences, appointment booking, reactivation campaigns, review requests, missed-call text-back, pipeline automations

Design trigger logic that handles real-world edge cases — duplicate contacts, multi-source leads, status changes, conditional branching

Set up and maintain conversion tracking, custom fields, tags, and pipeline logic that actually reflects how each client's business works

Handle A2P 10DLC registrations end-to-end and troubleshoot rejections when they happen

Integrate GHL with external tools — Zapier, 
, webhooks, Twilio, Google Ads, Meta, and custom APIs

Build reusable snapshots so we can deploy proven systems across new clients fast

Analyze workflow performance — open rates, reply rates, booking rates, conversion drop-offs — and propose specific optimizations with data behind them

Write clear documentation and Loom walkthroughs of every system you build

Communicate proactively in Slack — flag issues before they become problems





Required Experience



Minimum 3 years working in GoHighLevel specifically — not just "marketing automation" in general

Deep mastery of workflows and trigger logic — you understand event triggers vs filters, wait conditions, if/else branches, and how to architect automations that don't break when reality gets messy

Proven A2P 10DLC track record — you've personally registered brands and campaigns and know how to get them approved (and what to do when they get rejected)

Hands-on experience with at least one US-based marketing agency

Strong understanding of GHL custom fields, tags, pipelines, calendars, conversation AI, and the full SMS/email infrastructure

Experience integrating GHL with external tools via webhooks and APIs

Ability to read and debug a workflow that someone else built without breaking it

Strong written English — most of our communication is async via Slack and Loom

Reliable internet and a setup that lets you do focused, quality work

Self-managing — you don't need someone watching over you to deliver





Nice to Have



HighLevel Certified Admin certification

Experience with home services, automotive, or local service business clients

Experience building GHL snapshots deployed across multiple sub-accounts

Basic HTML/CSS for landing page edits within GHL

Familiarity with Google Ads, Meta Ads, and how lead data flows back into GHL





How We Work

This is a part-time role with real flexibility. We don't care when you work — we care that the work gets done well, that you respond to Slack within a reasonable window during your active days, and that deadlines get hit. Some of our team works mornings, some works nights. As long as you're communicating and delivering, we don't track hours.

What we do expect:



Active on Slack during the days you're working — not radio silence for 3 days

Hit agreed deadlines, or flag early if something is going to slip

Self-manage your workload — we'll give you priorities, you figure out your schedule

Show up to a weekly team check-in (we'll find a time that works for you)



If you need rigid structure and someone telling you what to do hour by hour, this isn't the right fit. If you do your best work when trusted to manage your own time, you'll thrive here.



How to Apply

We're not interested in copy-paste applications. Send us:



A 2–3 minute Loom video introducing yourself and walking us through the most complex GHL automation you've ever built. Show us the actual workflow on screen. Talk through your trigger logic.

A short written answer: "A client tells you their lead nurture sequence isn't working — replies are low and bookings have dropped 40% in the last 60 days. What are the first 5 things you check inside GHL, and why?"

A second short written answer: "Briefly describe how you handle A2P 10DLC registration and what you do when a campaign gets rejected."

Your hourly rate in USD and weekly availability.

Three GHL features or integrations you've built that you're proud of — one sentence each.



Applications without a Loom will not be reviewed. Generic applications go straight to the no pile. Strong candidates hear back within 48 hours.



What Working With Us Looks Like



Weekly pay via Wise (no waiting 30 days)

13th month bonus paid in December (pro-rated for part-time)

Performance bonuses tied to client retention and results

Real team — Slack, Loom, weekly check-ins, you're not a ghost

Long-term role with clear path to expanded responsibility as we scale

We invest in your tools — paid subscriptions, training, certifications

Genuine flexibility — we trust adults to manage their own time



We treat our team like partners. We expect the same in return.

title: Creative Branding Coordinator
description:
This is not a typical creative role



Claim Factory is an AI-powered auto damage appraisal platform. We are lean, systematized, and built differently than every other firm in this industry — and nobody knows it yet. That's the problem you're solving.



We are starting from zero. No logo. No website. No content library. No brand voice. Just an LLC, a superior product, and a founder who knows exactly what this industry is missing and is ready to say it out loud.



We need someone who can look at a blank canvas and see something. Someone who understands that brand is not just aesthetics — it's the story people tell about you when you're not in the room. Your job is to make sure that story is the right one, told consistently, across every surface Claim Factory touches.



You will have full creative autonomy. The founder will approve the vision — then you run it.



What you'll build



Phase 1 — Brand identity



Logo, color palette, typography, and visual system  ·  Brand voice guide — how we sound in every context  ·  Core messaging framework — what we say and what we never say  ·  Brand standards document that the entire team operates from



Phase 2 — Digital presence



Website — clean, simple, linked to our operating system (appraiser portal, client claim management, employee application)  ·  
pany page and founder presence  ·  Instagram and broader social presence built for industry authority, not vanity metrics



Phase 3 — Ongoing content engine



Consistent content across platforms that tells our story, educates the market, and builds trust with carriers, appraisers, and policyholders  ·  Content calendar, publishing cadence, and performance tracking  ·  Creative assets for sales, onboarding, and operations as the company scales



Who you are



You think in stories — not just posts, not just logos, but narratives that build over time

You have strong design instincts and can execute them, not just describe them

You understand B2B brand building — you know the difference between building authority and chasing likes

You can write — clearly, compellingly, and in a voice that isn't generic

You are self-directed — you don't wait to be told what to post next week

You are hungry — you want to build something you can point to and say you made that

You are consistent — brand authority is built in years, not campaigns

Tools & platforms in scope



Figma or equivalent

Canva (advanced)

LinkedIn

Instagram

Website (no-code or light dev)

Adobe Creative Suite (nice to have)

Content scheduling tools

Loom or video editing basics

What we are not looking for



We are not looking for someone to post generic content on a schedule. We are not looking for someone to copy what every other IA firm does — "we have appraisers everywhere and our portal accepts your file." That is the industry floor, not the ceiling.



Claim Factory's process is our product. Our transparency is our differentiator. Our story has never been told. We need someone who gets that and can translate it into a brand that makes carriers, appraisers, and policyholders feel like they found something different — because they did.

title: AIRBNB PROPERTU MANAGER
description:
Commitment: Full-time (8 hours per day, 7 days per week)

Start Date: ASAP



About the Role:

We are a growing short-let property business based in the UK, managing multiple Airbnb listings. We are looking for a reliable and experienced Virtual Assistant (VA) to fully support and manage the day-to-day operations of our Airbnb properties.



This is a full-time role that requires a daily commitment (7 days a week), strong communication skills, and a solid understanding of the short-let rental business. You will be a key part of our team, ensuring smooth operations and excellent guest experiences.



Please note: This must be your only job. We require full attention and availability to support our operations.



Key Responsibilities:



Daily Tasks:



* Respond promptly to guest messages and booking inquiries on Airbnb and other platforms

* Confirm bookings and monitor availability calendars

* Review and update front-end listings daily to ensure accurate pricing, visibility, and bookability

* Coordinate with cleaning and maintenance teams to ensure properties are guest-ready

* Monitor guest check-ins and check-outs, ensuring smooth transitions and issue resolution

* Track and report daily task completion, guest interactions, and progress on ongoing issues

* Ensure keys and access details are properly managed and communicated



Weekly Tasks:



* Review upcoming reservations and coordinate logistics

* Follow up with vendors and cleaners for feedback and issue resolution

* Ensure supplies and consumables are stocked or restocked where needed

* Update guest manuals, listing descriptions, and SOPs if necessary

* Prepare and send weekly reports summarizing operations, occupancy, and performance



Monthly Tasks:



* Track KPIs such as occupancy rates, reviews, and guest satisfaction

* Assist with monthly expenses and supplier/vendor tracking

* Identify recurring problems and propose solutions to improve efficiency

* Contribute to operational improvements and SOP updates



Additional Responsibilities:



* Assist in sourcing new short-let units and growing the business

* Provide basic support with social media marketing and online visibility

* Support the business owner with general administrative tasks as needed



Requirements:



* At least 1 year of experience working as a VA for Airbnb or a short-let property management business

* Excellent written and spoken English

* Strong organisational and time-management skills

* Proficient with tools like Google Drive, Google Sheets, and online communication platforms

* Familiar with Airbnb (calendar, messaging, resolution centre, etc.)

* Self-motivated, dependable, and able to handle tasks independently

* Flexibility to handle urgent matters, especially during peak guest hours

* Must not have other job commitments; this is a dedicated full-time role



Working Hours:



* 8 hours per day, 7 days a week

* Flexible schedule, but availability must align with UK guest needs (mornings, afternoons, or evenings as required)

* Occasional urgent requests may need handling outside regular hours



What We Offer:



* Long-term role with room for growth

* Clear documentation and training provided

* Supportive and collaborative work environment

* Potential for bonuses and pay increases based on performance



To Apply:

Please send your CV and a short cover letter explaining your experience with Airbnb and why you're a good fit for this role. Only applicants with direct experience in short-let or Airbnb property management will be considered.

title: Video Content Creator / Remote Assistant (Short-Form Content for Career Brand)

description:

Salary: $800 – $1,200/month USD 

Start Date: Immediate



About Us

We're LMD Growth Agency, a digital marketing agency based in South Florida. We run paid social, paid search, SEO, and web development for a portfolio of established US clients across home services, automotive, healthcare, and retail. Our clients aren't startups — they're real businesses spending real ad dollars and expecting real results.

We're looking for a creative who lives and breathes Meta ads, has serious taste, and is already shipping work using the new wave of AI video and image tools. If you're still relying on stock footage and Canva templates, this isn't for you. If you're producing AI-driven UGC ads in Arcads, generating ad concepts in Midjourney, and editing in CapCut Pro at speed, keep reading.



What You'll Actually Do



Concept and produce Meta ads 
 + Instagram) for multiple clients across different verticals — static ads, short-form video (15s, 30s), AI UGC, and motion graphics

Use AI tools to dramatically increase creative volume and test velocity — Midjourney, Runway, Kling, Sora/Veo, Arcads, HeyGen, ElevenLabs, Adobe Firefly, CapCut Pro

Study what's currently winning on Meta in our clients' verticals and bring fresh angles, hooks, and formats every week

Produce ad variations at scale — different hooks, different visuals, different CTAs — so we can test fast and find winners

Work directly from creative briefs and reference ads we share, but bring your own ideas to the table — we want a creative partner, not an order-taker

Edit and assemble UGC-style video ads from raw client footage and AI-generated assets

Maintain brand consistency across multiple clients while adapting tone and style to each one

Communicate proactively in Slack — share work-in-progress, ask smart questions, flag what's working





Required Experience



Minimum 3 years producing performance creative for Meta ads specifically — not general social media content, not branding work

Demonstrable AI tool fluency — you're already using Midjourney, Runway, Kling, Arcads, HeyGen, or ElevenLabs in your actual workflow, not just experimenting on weekends

Strong portfolio of Meta ads you've personally produced — with metrics on at least a few of them (CTR, hook rate, ROAS, or similar)

Mastery of CapCut Pro for fast video assembly and editing

Solid command of Photoshop, Illustrator, or Figma for static ad design

Real understanding of what makes a Meta ad stop the scroll — hooks, pacing, captions, pattern interrupts, UGC conventions

Ability to produce ads quickly without sacrificing quality — speed is part of the job

Strong written English for Slack communication and ad copy iteration

Self-managing — you don't need someone watching over you to deliver





Nice to Have



Experience producing ads for home services, automotive, healthcare, or local service businesses

Direct experience inside Meta Ads Manager (reading performance data, not just delivering files)

Voiceover scripting experience for AI delivery (ElevenLabs, HeyGen)

Motion graphics skills (After Effects or equivalent)

Light copywriting ability — primary headlines, captions, CTAs





How We Work

This is a part-time role with real flexibility. We don't care when you work — we care that the work gets done well, that you respond to Slack within a reasonable window during your active days, and that deadlines get hit. Some of our team works mornings, some works nights. As long as you're communicating and delivering, we don't track hours.

What we do expect:



Active on Slack during the days you're working — not radio silence for 3 days

Hit agreed deadlines, or flag early if something is going to slip

Self-manage your workload — we'll give you priorities, you figure out your schedule

Show up to a weekly team check-in (we'll find a time that works for you)



If you need rigid structure and someone telling you what to do hour by hour, this isn't the right fit. If you do your best work when trusted to manage your own time, you'll thrive here.

title: Finance Manager

Coordinating contractor payments for a 40+ person team. Building reports that help leadership make smarter decisions.

What You'll Do:

mbers

Bonus Points:

You've worked in a fast-paced startup or scaling company



This Role Is NOT For...

People who only do data entry without understanding the bigger picture

Those who wait to be asked for reports instead of proactively sharing insights

Anyone who struggles with accuracy or makes careless errors with numbers

People who can't explain financial concepts simply to non-finance tea
mbers

Those who've only worked in slow-moving corporate environments

People who see finance as just "tracking the past" instead of informing the future



Compensation & Benefits:

Plus:

Quarterly profit pool distribution (2-7.5% of company profits shared among team)

Remote-first environment

Work with a team of A-players who actually give a damn

Direct founder access—no layers of bureaucracy

Real ownership of your domain—we don't micromanage

We don't waste time—yours or ours. Here's how it works:

Step 2: Initial Interview with Founder

Step 3: Internal Assessment by Rescale

Step 4: WhatsApp update for next steps

title: Sales & Tech Representative
description:
We’re Hiring!



Are you a skilled salesperson with a background in gas plumbing and/or electrical work? Do you enjoy helping customers find the right solution while using your technical knowledge? Join our growing team and work with a US online home goods retailer that values expertise, professionalism, and customer satisfaction.





Position:

Sales & Tech Representative – Technical Products (Gas & Electrical Focus)

Location: Remote (Philippines-based)

Schedule: Monday-Friday, 11:00 AM to 7:00 PM EST (40 hours/week)

Salary: Competitive + Performance Bonuses





About the Role:

As a Salesperson, you will assist customers with product selection, provide technical support during the sales process, and close sales with confidence. You’ll work with gas fireplaces, heaters, stoves, and other appliances, so previous hands-on experience or technical knowledge of gas or electrical systems is a MUST.





Job Duties and Responsibilities:

Receive incoming phone calls and live chats from potential customers (prospects) who indicated that they need shopping assistance. This does not include calls and chats from existing customers that need help with their orders (that’s a job for a different department)

Help customers choose the right gas or electric heating appliance for their needs

Makes follow-up calls, writes follow-up
s, and text messages to potential buyers. We try to follow up 5x with each person that contacted us (unless they indicate that they are no longer interested). We don’t do cold calling. Only reaching out to prospects who interacted with our online store and left their contact information.

After each interaction with a prospect/customer, writes notes & comments in our CRM (Asana)

Communicate efficiently and build rapport with potential customers

Assists prospects in their shopping journey and helps make the purchase decision

Ensures that all prospects’ questions and objections are addressed

Leads every interaction to a close sale, which means an order is placed on our online store

Prepares custom quotes, price matches competitors if necessary, and offers custom discounts. This requires calculating margins & ensuring profitability.

Answering questions about the products we offer, including technical questions. Learns the specifications, features and benefits of our bestsellers. Over time, becomes an expert in the industry.

Explain technical specifications and installation requirements in simple terms

If not able to answer product-specific questions, conducts research in the materials provided by the supplier, or reaches out to the supplier to get the information

Confirms stock availability and lead times when offering products to prospects

Documents standard procedures and best practices in a written form, so that other tea
mbers can learn from them





Requirements:

Sales Experience – 2+ years, preferably with B2C or B2B technical products

Gas Plumbing or Electrical Experience – Formal training or work experience (installer, technician, or similar roles preferred)

Excellent English – Both written and spoken

Confident Communicator – Can explain complex concepts to non-technical customers

Tech-Savvy – Comfortable using CRMs,
 platforms, order tools, etc.

Reliable and Proactive – Takes initiative and works independently

Home Office Setup – Stable internet, noise-free environment, backup power is a plus





Preferred but Not Required:

Knowledge of venting, HVAC, or hearth appliances

US product or code familiarity

Experience in the construction or home improvement industry





Why Join Us?

Sales commission: 8% of gross profit from all orders closed. Gross profit means the sale amount minus the product and shipping cost. Paid monthly. No upper limit. Monthly commission payouts range between $1,200 (the guaranteed minimum) and as high as $3,000 (for the top performer). The average monthly payout is $1,465 (average from the last 2 years).

$1,200/month minimum guaranteed pay

15 days paid days off per year (also convertible to min. guaranteed commission)

Paid training on all aspects of the role

Opportunities for professional development

Friendly atmosphere

Online Interview process

Fully remote position





How to Apply:

Apply through OnlineJobs.ph with the subject line: "Sales Pro - [Your Name]"

page views

button clicks

scroll or visual state changes

Meta Pixel Helper

Meta Test Events

I need all this done within the next 10 hours! 



You will be doing it for 2 funnels in GHL.

title: Urgent: Product Research & Sourcing Expert (Remote)
description:
At Skin Research Institute (SRI), we specialize in creating high-performance beauty and wellness tools designed to make a real impact. From advanced skincare to haircare technology, our products are backed by science and built for results. Sold both direct-to-consumer and via Amazon, our brand is trusted by women worldwide. As we continue to grow, we're looking for a strategic Product Research & Sourcing Expert with a marketing mindset to help us scale and innovate faster.



Job Summary

We’re seeking a Product Research & Sourcing Expert who is not only experienced in supplier and manufacturing coordination, but also has a strong grasp of brand positioning, consumer trends, and marketing strategy. In this role, you’ll work closely with cross-functional teams to source products and packaging that align with our Unique Selling Propositions (USPs) and meet evolving market demands.



Primary Duties and Responsibilities

Product & R&D Timeline Management: Maintain an always-updated product development snapshot to monitor manufacturing status and proactively avoid delays.

Pretotyping & Trend Scouting: Collaborate with factories to explore new product categories and innovations that support product-market fit and deliver fresh USPs.

Quality Control Coordination: Ensure quality standards are met and sustained through consistent communication with suppliers and product testing.

Sourcing & Procurement: Identify and vet new suppliers, source competitive quotes, negotiate MOQ, pricing, and lead times.

Packaging & Brand Alignment: Source packaging that enhances brand perception and strengthens product USPs through aesthetics and functionality.

Spec Sheet & Tech Pack Oversight: Work with factories to maintain up-to-date product documentation, including part numbers, materials, and contract terms.

Cost Optimization: Track and manage per-part pricing and production efficiency without compromising quality or brand positioning.

Collaborate with Marketing: Work with product and marketing teams to ensure sourced products clearly communicate their value proposition and unique benefits.



Qualifications

2–5 years experience in sourcing, preferably in beauty, wellness, or consumer electronics

Familiar with Chinese and Asian suppliers, Alibaba, 1688, and related platforms

Marketing background or strong understanding of product positioning and USP development

Experience working with DTC brands or Amazon sellers is a strong plus

Strong written and spoken English; Mandarin is a bonus

High attention to detail; process-driven and self-managed

Excellent communication, negotiation, and supplier relationship skills

Comfortable working in a fast-paced, growth-focused environment



What We Offer

Competitive salary – earn in USD

Health coverage (HMO) – after one year

Paid time off – we believe in work-life balance

Global exposure – work with a diverse and innovative team



Explore Our Brands

title: WordPress Technical SEO Specialist Needed (Rank Math + Schema + Core Web Vitals Fixes)
description:
Fix Structured Data, Canonicals, H1 Issues + Improve CWV Performance



Hi! My name is Rhett and I'm the Marketing Manager at The Equine Practice Company 
/ (If you see a recent post on this, I have cancelled that task and made this project clearer to ensure there is no confusion on what we need you to do)



I’m looking for an experienced Technical SEO Specialist to help fix several technical issues identified that we have with our website.



The Equine Practice Company - a continuing education platform for equine veterinarians worldwide.



Let me make this very clear before you apply.



- This is not a content role or keyword research role.

- This is a hands-on technical implementation project focused on:



- Structured data (Schema / JSON-LD)

- Entity linking (Organization ? Speakers)

- Canonical + redirect consistency

- Core Web Vitals performance improvements



This is a one-time technical SEO implementation project. I'd prefer a fixed-price structure with clear milestones. If the work is done well, I may offer ongoing technical SEO support for future schema + performance needs to this website and our second website.



I want someone who can fix this properly and document the work clearly.



A. Current Website Setup



To save time, here is our technical environment:



Website platform: WordPress

Theme: Kadence

SEO plugin: Rank Math

Hosting: WPX (Cloudflare available)

Canonical preference: I don’t mind www vs non-www - I just want one version enforced consistently



Key Tasks



1. Schema & Structured Data Implementation (High Priority) We currently have gaps in structured data, and we need a clean schema setup using best practices.



You will implement:



- Organization schema site-wide

- AboutPage schema

- ContactPage schema + ContactPoint

- Person schema for our “Meet the Speaker” pages

- Proper entity linking (Organization ? Website ? Speakers)



I want a hybrid approach:



- Rank Math for baseline schema

- Custom JSON-LD where needed (especially for Speaker Person schema)



Important: Avoid duplicate/conflicting schema output.



All schema must be validated using:



- Google Rich Results Test

- 
 Validator



2. Homepage SEO Fix (H1 Hierarchy) - Our homepage currently has two H1 tags, which causes structural confusion for Google.



You will:



- Fix the heading hierarchy (single clean H1)

- Keep design intact (Kadence blocks)



Suggested H1:



“The Equine Practice Company – Continuing Education for Equine Veterinarians”



3. Canonical + Domain Consistency (WWW vs Non-WWW) - We may have canonical duplication issues.



You will:



- Audit canonical tags across templates

- Enforce one preferred domain version via proper 301 redirects

- Ensure Rank Math canonicals match server/CDN redirects

- Confirm consistent indexing in Google Search Console



4. Core Web Vitals + Performance Improvements (CWV) - Because this is a membership-based site, performance matters.



I need help improving:



- LCP (Largest Contentful Paint)

- TTFB (Time to First Byte)

- INP / CLS issues if present



You will:



- Identify the top causes of poor CWV (mobile-first)

- Optimize heavy scripts/plugins where possible

- Recommend hosting/CDN improvements if needed



Retest using:



- PageSpeed Insights

- Lighthouse

- CrUX data



It is important that you document before/after results.



Most Importantly, The Final Deliverables (What You Must Provide)



At completion of this project for our website, I expect:



- Working JSON-LD schema for Organization, AboutPage, ContactPage, Person

- Clean entity graph 

- Validation proof (screenshots or links)

- Canonical + redirect mapping summary

- Before/after homepage H1 structure proof

- Core Web Vitals improvement report

- Clear documentation of everything changed



Who Is My Ideal Candidate For This Project - I'm looking for someone with:



- Strong WordPress technical SEO experience

- Deep schema + JSON-LD knowledge (not just plugins)

- Experience with Rank Math + Kadence themes

- Understanding of Core Web Vitals (TTFB/LCP)

- Ability to work independently and document clearly



If you are a real technical implementer (not just an SEO auditor), I’d love to work with you.



 Please answer the following questions when submitting a proposal:



1.What schema does Rank Math output by default, and how do you avoid duplication when adding custom Person schema?

2. Where would you enforce the canonical domain version (WordPress vs Cloudflare vs server)?

3. Share one example of Organization + Person JSON-LD you have implemented before (client details can be hidden).



I'd prefer a fixed-price structure with clear milestones. What is your fixed price to complete this project and break it down in milestones.



Please estimate how long this will take to fully complete the project.

title: Web Designer & Developer (Virtual Assistant)
description:
About the Role



We are a growing agency looking for a talented and reliable Web Designer & Developer to join our remote team. In this role, you will be the creative and technical engine behind our clients' websites, designing beautiful mockups in Figma and bringing them to life on WordPress. You will also support clients on Wix and Squarespace with ongoing updates and maintenance.

This is a full-time position with consistent work, clear communication, and a supportive team. We're looking for someone who takes ownership of their work, communicates proactively, and delivers high-quality output on time.



What You'll Be Doing



• Design professional website mockups in Figma (primarily 2 mockups per project, desktop and mobile views)

• Develop and build websites on WordPress based on approved Figma designs

• Perform ongoing website updates and edits for clients on Wix and Squarespace

• Ensure websites are responsive, fast-loading, and cross-browser compatible

• Implement basic on-page SEO best practices during development

• Communicate project progress and flag any blockers or timeline concerns proactively

• Revise designs and builds based on client and internal feedback

• Maintain organized project files and documentation



Requirements



Design Skills



• Strong proficiency in Figma, you can design clean, modern, and client-ready mockups independently

• Good eye for layout, typography, and visual hierarchy

• Experience designing for both desktop and mobile

• Experience in graphic design, creating ads for social media when needed



Development Skills



• Solid experience building websites on WordPress (themes, Elementor or similar page builders, custom CSS)

• Comfortable making updates and edits on Wix and Squarespace

• Basic knowledge of HTML and CSS

• Ability to translate a Figma design into a pixel-accurate website build



Work Style & Communication



• Fluent in written English, clear, professional communication is a must

• Reliable with deadlines and able to manage your own time effectively

• Comfortable using tools like Slack, Trello, Asana, or similar project management platforms

• Responds to messages within business hours and proactively communicates updates

Nice to Have (Not Required)

• Experience with WooCommerce or basic e-commerce setups

• Familiarity with Google Analytics, Search Console, or basic SEO tools

• Experience creating amazing graphic designs in Canva or Adobe tools

• Knowledge of website speed optimization (image compression, caching plugins, etc.)



What We Offer



• $7/hr Paid Semi Monthly 15th and last day of the month (tracked)

• Full-time, fully remote position, work from wherever you are

• Stable, ongoing work with a consistent flow of projects

• Supportive team environment with clear briefs and feedback

• Long-term opportunity for the right candidate, we're not looking for short-term help



How to Apply



If you think you are the right person for this role, please create and send us a 1-minute video introducing yourself, sharing your background, and explaining why you would be the best person for this role. Please also forward your resume and your earliest available start date to Contact at umteencompany dot com. Candidates will not be interviewed if you do not send a 1-minute video.

title: Sales Assistant
description:
Now Hiring — Transfer Agent + Customer Care Specialist (Solar Enrollment)

We're hiring for two distinct phone-based roles. Both are remote, part-time, long-term, and require high-level spoken English.



Timeline (must be available)

Apply + voice recording: ASAP

Trial shift: Friday May 1 – Saturday May 2

Hiring decision: Saturday May 2

Paid training: Saturday May 2 – Sunday May 3

Live start date: Monday, May 4

Only apply if you can commit to this timeline.



About us

Community solar enrollment company helping US homeowners save on their electricity bills. Steady flow of warm inbound leads from opt-in forms.



Role 1: Transfer Agent (2 openings)

What you'll do: Run a 3-line power dialer (Wave Dialer), calling warm inbound leads who didn't connect with our AI on the first attempt. On pickup: deliver a 30–45 second greeting, confirm interest, warm-transfer the lead to a US-based closer. You do NOT close the sale. Your job is to qualify and transfer.



Hours & pay:



$6–$10/hr USD (final rate based on English fluency assessed during the paid trial shift), paid bi-weekly via Wise/PayPal

30 hours/week, 6 days/week (Mon–Sat)

Choose one shift on US Central Time:

Shift A — Morning: 10am–3pm CT

Shift B — Evening: 3pm–8pm CT

Dialing volume: ~150 dials/hour on a 3-line power dialer.



Role 2: Customer Care Specialist (1 opening)

What you'll do: Handle post-enrollment customer interactions and assist on three-way calls during the qualification process:



Welcome calls with newly enrolled customers

Three-way calls with the customer + Lifeline (when qualification needs verification or temp password)

Three-way calls with the customer + utility companies (account verification, payment arrangements)

Document outcomes in our CRM (GoHighLevel)

Hours & pay:



$6–$10/hr USD (final rate based on English fluency assessed during the paid trial shift), paid bi-weekly via Wise/PayPal

30 hours/week, Mon–Fri, ~9am–3pm CT (may flex by 1–2 hours)

Required for BOTH roles

High-level English with very minimal / near-native accent. You will be on calls with US homeowners and US government agencies — clear, fluent, easy-to-understand English is non-negotiable.

1+ year customer-facing phone experience (outbound, customer service, or call center)

Patient demeanor (especially for Role 2 — government three-way calls can run long)

Detail-oriented; able to take notes and enter data into a CRM in real time

Reliable wired internet (25+ Mbps)

USB noise-cancelling headset

Quiet workspace

Stable PC/laptop (Windows or Mac)

Available during US Central Time hours

Long-term commitment (6+ months)

Available for the timeline above (live by Mon May 4)

Bonus (not required)

Solar / energy / utility industry experience

GoHighLevel (GHL) experience

For Role 1: Wave Dialer / Wavv / Mojo / ReadyMode experience

For Role 2: Experience with US government benefits programs (Lifeline, LIHEAP, Medicaid, SNAP, SSI)

What we provide

All necessary tools (Wave Dialer, GoHighLevel CRM, Slack)

Pre-loaded lead lists / scripts / call procedures

Onboarding + paid training (1–2 days)

2-hour paid trial shift before final hire (final pay rate set after trial)

How to apply

60-second voice recording of you reading this script: "Hi, this is [your name] calling from Saveyourenergy. I'm following up on the form you filled out about lowering your electricity bill through community solar. Do you have just a 
nt? Great — let me connect you with one of our solar specialists right now."

Indicate which role you're applying for:

Role 1: Transfer Agent — Shift A (10am–3pm CT)

Role 1: Transfer Agent — Shift B (3pm–8pm CT)

Role 2: Customer Care Specialist (9am–3pm CT, Mon–Fri)

Open to either (we'll match you based on fit)

Briefly describe the most complex customer-facing phone situation you've handled and how you resolved it

Confirm you can start by Monday, May 4 (trial shift Fri May 1 / Sat May 2; training Sat-Sun)

title: Acquisition Lead Manager
description:
Property Seller Solutions is hiring a driven and disciplined outbound lead manager to generate new real estate opportunities through proactive cold calling and database follow-up.



This is a revenue-generating role focused on outbound activity, lead qualification, and appointment setting. The right candidate will be comfortable making a high volume of calls, navigating rejection, and building rapport quickly over the phone.



Key Responsibilities

- Make outbound calls to new and aged lead lists

- Identify property owners’ motivation, timeline, and decision-making authority

- Pre-qualify opportunities based on company criteria

- Set qualified appointments for acquisitions team

- Maintain accurate CRM documentation

- Follow structured call cadences and follow-up systems

- Assist in reactivating dormant opportunities in the database



Qualifications

- Strong verbal communication skills (solid English skills are required)

- High level of confidence on the phone

- Comfortable asking direct financial and property-related questions

- Highly organized and disciplined with follow-up

- Able to meet daily activity metrics and performance expectations and provide daily activity reports

- Prior phone sales or call center experience preferred

- Real estate experience is a plus but not required



Performance Expectations

- 300+ outbound dials per day (subject to adjustments)

- Consistent talk time and meaningful conversations

- Appointment and qualification targets tied to performance bonuses



Compensation Structure

- Bonuses tied to qualified appointments and deal performance

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a production-based environment and wants a clear path to higher earning potential. Ongoing training with elite cold callers will be provided for optimal performance based on lead type and best practices.



As part of our hiring process, we require all candidates to complete a Predictive Index (PI) assessment before moving forward to interviews. This helps us better understand your strengths and how you might fit within our team. We will contact those who are a great fit for this type of role.

title: Cold Caller / Appointment Setter
description:
Axisbridge Medical Staffing is a fast growing healthcare BPO placing HIPAA-compliant virtual medical billers and administrators with independent US healthcare practices. We work exclusively with the US market and our clients are preferably small to medium sized independent medical practice owners, office managers, and billing directors across mental health, chiropractic, physical therapy, and primary care.



We are hiring one elite cold caller who has spent years on the phone, knows exactly how to navigate gatekeepers, handle objections, and book qualified appointments with busy medical professionals.



What you will do:

Call independent US healthcare practices from a verified, pre-researched lead list you will create from a database we will provide 

Follow a proven script to uncover billing and administrative pain points

Book qualified discovery calls directly into the closer's calendar

Log every call, outcome, and follow-up note in a simple CRM tracker

Follow up on warm prospects who did not book on the first call

Hit a minimum target of 50 outbound calls per day

Provide daily call reports with outcomes, objections encountered, and booking rate



What you earn:

$3 per hour base: paid weekly

$150 bonus for every client that signs from your booked call

Your guaranteed base is $360 per month

One close per month brings your total to $510

Two closes per month brings your total to $710

Three closes per month brings your total to $910

Four closes per month brings your total to $1,110

No ceiling on commission — every client you help close adds $200 to your earnings



Minimum requirements:

Minimum 5 years experience in outbound cold calling or appointment setting

Minimum 3 years experience specifically calling US-based clients — healthcare, insurance, or medical industry strongly preferred

Proven track record of hitting or exceeding daily call targets and booking quotas

Deep experience handling common sales objections — not interested, we already have someone, send me an
, call me back later

Experience navigating gatekeepers to reach decision makers — practice owners, office managers, billing directors

Previous BPO experience in a US-facing healthcare or medical role is strongly preferred

Excellent spoken English with a clear neutral accent — US clients must be able to understand you effortlessly on the first call

Strong professional telephone manner and the ability to build rapport quickly with busy medical professionals

Reliable high-speed internet connection, noise-cancelling headset, and a quiet professional working environment

Available Monday to Friday during US Central or Eastern business hours

Self-managed, disciplined, and comfortable working independently without supervision

Experience working with a CRM or call tracking system

You are the right person if:

You have spent years making calls and you know exactly how to get a decision maker on the phone, uncover their pain, and book the next step. You back yourself completely to perform and you want to be paid based on results not hours. You understand the US healthcare market, you speak confidently with medical professionals, and you treat every call as an opportunity to help a practice solve a real problem.



To apply you must send the following, incomplete applications will not be considered:

Your CV with specific cold calling and appointment setting experience clearly detailed including industries called, years of experience, and average daily call volume.
